blocks:
Ayahs

طه (۱)
Ta Ha! (1)
مَآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ لِتَشْقَىٰٓ (۲)
We did not send down to you the Quran that you should be miserable, (2)
إِلَّا تَذْكِرَةً لِّمَن يَخْشَىٰ (۳)
but only as an admonition to him who fears [his Lord]. (3)
تَنزِيلًا مِّمَّنْ خَلَقَ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ ٱلْعُلَى (٤)
A sending down [of the Revelation] from Him who created the earth and the lofty heavens (4)

Key Points

Various meanings and interpretations have been stated for the disconnected letters (حروف مقطّعه) "طه"; among them are that it is a Name of Allah, or a word of oath, or the name of the Prophet. (2) Just as the Ahl al-Bayt (Household) of the Prophet are called "Āl Tāhā," or Hadrat Ali عليه السلام is called "Tāhā," (3) and in Du'a Nudbah, we address Imam al-Zaman عليه السلام as "Yabna Tāhā" (O Son of Tāhā). In any case, these letters are not superfluous or meaningless, and throughout the long period since the revelation of the Qur'an, and despite the existence of many linguistic enemies, no noteworthy objection or flaw has been raised regarding this matter so far. (4) The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, due to His love, fervor, and commitment to fulfilling His duty, became restless, "لتشقى" (that you should be distressed), to the extent that in performing His duty, His feet would swell and His blessed complexion would turn pale. (5) "تنزیل" (Tanzīl) means gradual revelation. That is, the Qur'an was revealed over a period of 23 years during various events and occasions, and this does not contradict the instantaneous revelation (نزول دفعى) of the Qur'an upon the blessed heart of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله on the Night of Qadr (Laylat al-Qadr).

2) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 3) Bihar, Vol. 97, p. 375. 4) Tafsir Namuneh. 5) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- Divine revelation was not sent down to cause hardship and distress. "ما انزلنا...لتشقى"

2- In fulfilling religious duties, enduring unbearable hardships is not necessary. "ما انزلنا...لتشقى"

3- Man inherently possesses beliefs, but because he becomes heedless of them, he needs reminder. "تَذكرة"

4- Piety (Taqwa), knowledge, and awe (Khashyah) are concomitant. (6) "تَذكرة لمَن یخشى"

5- For proper growth, the perfection of the educator and preacher alone is not enough; the ground for acceptance must also be prepared. "لمَن یخشى" Just as good seed, suitable land, and care are necessary for a good harvest.

6- A sense of responsibility is the prerequisite for accepting advice. "تَذكرة لمَن یخشى"

7- The duty of the Prophet is to remind, not to compel people to accept. "الاّ تَذكرة لمَن یخشى"

8- Legislation (Tashrī') and creation (Takwīn) both originate from a single source, and their laws correspond with one another. "تنزیلاً ممّن خلق"

9- If the Creator of the magnificent heavens speaks, that speech is extremely important. "تنزیلاً ممّن خلق السموات العُلى..." (The speech of the Commander is the commander of speech.)

6) As we read in other verses: "تذكرة للمتّقین" (Haqqah, 48), "انّما یخشى اللّه من عباده العلماء" (Fatir, 28).

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Taha

Surah Taha, with 135 verses, is a Makkan Surah, and its content, like other Makkan Surahs, mostly speaks about the Origin (Mabda') and the Return (Ma'ad). This Surah is the first Surah that details the story of Hadrat Moses عليه السلام, dedicating approximately eighty verses to it. A part of it also concerns the greatness of the Qur'an and the Attributes of Allah, and another part relates to the story of Adam and Eve and the temptation of Satan, concluding with awakening admonitions. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: Whoever perseveres in reciting Surah Taha will be beloved by Allah, and on the Day of Resurrection, his book of deeds will be given to him in his right hand. (1)

1) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Ayahs

ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ ٱسْتَوَىٰ (٥)
—the All-beneficent, settled on the Throne. (5)
لَهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا وَمَا تَحْتَ ٱلثَّرَىٰ (٦)
To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and whatever is between them, and whatever is under the ground. (6)
وَإِن تَجْهَرْ بِٱلْقَوْلِ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَعْلَمُ ٱلسِّرَّ وَأَخْفَى (۷)
Whether you speak loudly [or in secret tones,] He indeed knows the secret and what is still more hidden. (7)
ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ لَهُ ٱلْأَسْمَآءُ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ (۸)
Allah—there is no god except Him—to Him belong the Best Names. (8)

Key Points

In these few verses, four prominent attributes of Allah are presented: A: Creatorship. «خَلق الارض والسّموات» B: Sovereignty. «على العَرش استوى » C: Ownership. «له ما فى السّموات و ما...» D: Omniscience. «یعلم السّر و اَخفى»

The Lord's dominion over the Throne (al-Arsh), which is mentioned in other Surahs of the Quran with the phrase «اِستوى على العرش», is a metaphor for Allah's encompassment and sovereignty over the universe. For example, when it is said in Persian: so-and-so sat upon the throne, or so-and-so was pulled down from the throne, it means he became the ruler or that power and governance were taken from him. Usually, along with the phrase «اِستوى على العرش» and its equivalents, a sentence follows which serves as its interpretation, such as: «ثمّ استوى على العرش یغشى اللیل النهار»(7) He has dominion over the Throne and covers the day with the night; «ثمّ استوى على العرش یُدبّر الامر»(8) He encompasses existence and manages the affairs; «ثمّ استوى على العرش یعلم ما یلج فى الارض و ما...»(9) He has dominion over existence and knows everything; «ثمّ استوى على العرش مالكم من دونه من ولّى»(10) He has dominion over the Throne and there is no guardian besides Him; and in this verse: «على العرش استوى له ما فى السموات و ما فى الارض»

The word «ثَرى» means moist earth, and «تحت الثرى» (under the moist earth) includes the dead, treasures, and everything else hidden in the soil.

«اسماء حُسنى» (the Most Beautiful Names) are the attributes of Allah, which, according to narrations, number 99 names, and if Allah is invoked in supplication by these names, it brings abundant blessings and effects. The Divine Names are not limited to these, and in some supplications, such as Dua Jawshan Kabir, up to a thousand names are mentioned. Of course, seeking intercession through these names is not merely memorizing and reciting them, but one must strive to approach those attributes oneself.

The singular form of «الحسنى» (al-Husna), despite its noun «اسماء» (Asma) being plural, is perhaps due to the implied word «هذه» (these), meaning «هذه الاسماء الحسنى» (These are the Most Beautiful Names), although there are other examples in the Quran, such as: «مآرب اخرى»(11) and «حدائق ذات بهجة»(12).(13)

We read in narrations that the infallible Imams علیهم السلام have said: By Allah, we are the Divine Most Beautiful Names.(14)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «سرّ» (the secret) is that which you conceal in your heart, and «اخفى» (the most hidden) is that which has crossed your mind but you have forgotten it.(15) Meaning, it is not only hidden from others, but you yourself have become oblivious to it.

Allah is aware of everything, «یعلم السّر واَخفى». He knows the unseen of the heavens and the earth, «اعلم غیب السموات والارض»(16), He knows what every female carries, «یعلم ما تحمل كلّ اُنثى»(17), He knows the treachery of the eyes (the intent and meaning of glances), «یعلم خائنة الاعین»(18), He knows the thoughts that cross the heart, «یعلم ما فى قلوبكم»(19) and not a single leaf falls from a tree except that He knows it. «وما تسقط من وَرقة الاّ یعلمها»(20)

7) Al-A'raf, 54. 8) Yunus, 3. 9) Al-Hadid, 4. 10) As-Sajdah, 4. 11) Taha, 18. 12) An-Naml, 60. 13) Tafasir Namuneh, Majma' al-Bayan, Tabari, and Tibyan. 14) Tafsir Namuneh. 15) Tafsir Safi. 16) Al-Baqarah, 33. 17) Ar-Ra'd, 8. 18) Ghafir, 19. 19) Al-Ahzab, 51. 20) Al-An'am, 59.

Messages

1- Allah's dominion over existence is merciful, not tyrannical. «الرّحمن على العرش استوى»

2- The revelation of the Quran is a manifestation of Allah's Mercy and His authority over existence. «تنزیلا ممن خلق... الرّحمن على العرش استوى»

3- Allah's dominion over all of existence is uniform.(21) «على العرش استوى»

4- Allah is both the Creator of existence and its Sustainer and Ruler. «خلق ... على العرش استوى»

5- Allah has absolute knowledge and ownership over everything visible and invisible. «له ما فى السموات... و ما تحت الثّرى»

6- One way to prevent evil deeds is for people to pay attention to Divine knowledge regarding their hidden and manifest actions. «یعلم السّر وأخفى»

7- A good and beautiful name, both in wording and meaning, is a value, and Allah is both Beautiful Himself and possesses the best and most beautiful names. «له الاسماء الحُسنى»(21) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 128.

Ayahs

وَهَلْ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ مُوسَىٰٓ (۹)
Did the story of Moses come to you, (9)
إِذْ رَءَا نَارًا فَقَالَ لِأَهْلِهِ ٱمْكُثُوٓا۟ إِنِّىٓ ءَانَسْتُ نَارًا لَّعَلِّىٓ ءَاتِيكُم مِّنْهَا بِقَبَسٍ أَوْ أَجِدُ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ هُدًى (۱٠)
when he sighted a fire, and said to his family, ‘Wait! Indeed, I descry a fire! Maybe I will bring you a brand from it, or find some guidance at the fire.’ (10)

Key Points

Qabas (قبس) refers to a piece of fire that has been separated from its main body. In a section of the Surah, parts of the story of Moses (Musa) عليه السلام are detailed: A: The selection of Moses عليه السلام for prophethood (Risalah). B: His invitation to Pharaoh to accept the truth. C: His departure from Egypt and the drowning of Pharaoh. D: The calf-worship of the Children of Israel (Bani Israel). Moses عليه السلام set out on a journey with his pregnant wife and lost their way in the desert. In that dark desert, with cold and rainy weather, they needed fire for warmth and guidance. A fire became visible from afar, and the Prophet (Moses) headed toward that location to obtain fire and comfort his family. Suddenly, a call came, and Allah chose Moses for prophethood. (22) Yes, Moses عليه السلام went toward the fire to find a way of salvation for himself and his family, but Allah opened the path of salvation for others through him. He was seeking an earthly path, but God showed him the path of spirituality and felicity. He was pursuing individual guidance, but God presented Him with the path of guiding the Ummah (community).

22) That night was the night of Friday (Jumu'ah). Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- When relating historical facts, first make your audience eager to hear, and then narrate the event to them. «هل اتیك»

2- Asking questions is a suitable method for propagation (Tabligh) and education. «هل اتیك»

3- Narrating history provides an experiential admonition and a means for drawing lessons, and it comforts the heart and mind of the Prophet. «حدیث موسى» (In the culture of the Quran, hadith means history, not a new incident.)

4- Sometimes, a person sees the Divine Light in the form of fire. (23) «اذ راى ناراً»

5- Women have shared shoulder-to-shoulder with men in experiencing hardships. «لاهله»

6- Do not fear bitter events, because sometimes the grace of Allah manifests from the heart of seemingly unpleasant incidents. «اذ رأى ناراً»

7- The man is responsible for providing for the welfare of his family. «لعلّى اتیكم بقبس»

23) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Ayahs

فَلَمَّآ أَتَىٰهَا نُودِىَ يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ (۱۱)
So when he came to it, he was called, ‘O Moses! (11)
إِنِّىٓ أَنَا۠ رَبُّكَ فَٱخْلَعْ نَعْلَيْكَ ۖ إِنَّكَ بِٱلْوَادِ ٱلْمُقَدَّسِ طُوًى (۱۲)
Indeed I am your Lord! So take off your sandals. You are indeed in the sacred valley of Tuwa. (12)

Key Points

The name of Moses (Musa) appears in twenty Surahs and 136 times in the Quran, and the story of Moses عليه السلام is one of the greatest stories in the Quran, as it occupies approximately nine hundred verses of the Quran. The word "ربّ" (Rabb/Lord), which is one of the Beautiful Names of Allah, appears more than 970 times in the Quran, and Allah introduced Himself to Moses عليه السلام with this Name during the first manifestation. "انّى أنا ربّك" (Indeed, I am your Lord). And because the prophethood of Moses عليه السلام began with a divine call and speech, he is therefore called Kalim Allah (The one to whom Allah spoke). "و كَلَّم اللّه موسى تكليماً"(24) "وادى المقدّس" (The Sacred Valley) refers to a place far removed from fault and sin, and "طوى" (Tuwa) means something or a place that is mixed with blessing. In a Hadith, we read that the intention behind removing the two sandals is detaching the heart from two worries: one, the worry about the condition of his wife in that cold, and the other, the worry about the evil of Pharaoh.(25) Some have also considered it a reference to his wife and child, or the love of this world and the Hereafter. In the current Torah, the phrase "فاخلع نعليك" (Remove your sandals) is also mentioned with the same expression.(26)

24) An-Nisa, 164. 25) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn. 26) Torah, Book of Exodus, Chapter 3.

Messages

1- Often, a person takes a step intending one thing, but achieves a different result. «آتيكم منها بقَبَس... نُودى»

Moses went to bring fire in his hand, He saw a Fire that saved him from fire.

2- As soon as the Will of Allah is attached to the realization of something, all means come into the service of that goal. (The darkness of the night, the pregnancy of his wife, losing the way, seeing the fire—all became preliminaries for attracting Moses عليه السلام and presenting revelation and prophethood to him at the Divine rendezvous.)

3- Wherever disbelief is sensed, greater emphasis must be placed. «اِنّى انَا ربّك» (Indeed, I am your Lord.)

4- The signs of sanctity and spirituality of the task must also be evident in the appearance of individuals. «فاْخلع نعليك» (Remove your sandals.)

5- A deep relationship with Allah depends on detaching the heart from dependencies. «فاْخلَع نعليك» (Remove your sandals.)

6- Humility is one of the most basic requirements for receiving responsibilities. «فاخلع نعليك» (Remove your sandals.)

7- Some places possess a special sanctity. «فاخلع نعليك اِنّك بالواد المقدّس» (Remove your sandals, for indeed you are in the Sacred Valley.) (Going barefoot and removing shoes is among the etiquette of entering sacred places.)

8- Sacred messages descend in sacred places. «نُودىَ... بالواد المقدّس» (He was called... in the Sacred Valley.)

9- The acceptance of heavenly responsibilities has occurred in the pure heart of nature, such as Mount Tur, the Sacred Valley of Tuwa, and the Cave of Hira. «نُودىِ... بالواد المقدّس طُوى » (He was called... in the Sacred Valley of Tuwa.)

Ayahs

وَأَنَا ٱخْتَرْتُكَ فَٱسْتَمِعْ لِمَا يُوحَىٰٓ (۱۳)
I have chosen you; so listen to what is revealed. (13)
إِنَّنِىٓ أَنَا ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنَا۠ فَٱعْبُدْنِى وَأَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ لِذِكْرِىٓ (۱٤)
Indeed I am Allah—there is no god except Me. So worship Me, and maintain the prayer for My remembrance. (14)
إِنَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ ءَاتِيَةٌ أَكَادُ أُخْفِيهَا لِتُجْزَىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍۭ بِمَا تَسْعَىٰ (۱٥)
Indeed the Hour is bound to come: I will have it hidden, so that every soul may be rewarded for its endeavour. (15)

Key Points

One of the names of the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) is al-Sā'ah (the Hour). (27)

(27) This term, "Sā'ah," has been used more than forty times regarding the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah). Tafsir Furqan.

Messages

1- Prophets are appointed by Allah, not by the people. «انَا اخترتك»

2- The words of revelation must be heard with one's entire being, wholeheartedly. «فاستمع» (listen attentively), not «فاسمع» (just hear).

3- Among the etiquette of conversation is self-introduction. «اِنّنى انَا اللّه»

4- In the Divine system, knowledge is the basis of action. «اِنّنى انَا اللّه - فاعبدنى»

5- Knowing the fundamentals of religion (Usul al-Din) precedes performing the branches of religion (Furu' al-Din). «لااله الاّ انَا فاعبدنى»

Theoretical Monotheism (Tawhid Nazari) precedes practical Monotheism (Tawhid Amali). «اِنّنى انَا اللّه - فاعبدنى»

6- Worship must be sincere. The value of worship is measured by its sincerity. «فاعبدنى»

7- Monotheism (Tawhid) is at the head of all beliefs, and prayer (Salat) is at the head of all acts of worship. «لااله الاّ انا، فاعبدنى و أقم الصّلوة»

8- Prayer (Salat) is the first instruction and practical program for the prophets. «أقِم الصّلوة»

9- Prayer (Salat) existed in other religions as well. «أقم الصّلوة»

10- The goal of establishing prayer is not merely observing its form, but paying attention to its heart, which is the feeling of presence (of Allah). «أقم الصّلوة لذكرى»

11- Attention to the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) causes the establishment of prayer. «أقم الصّلوة - اُِنّ السّاعة آتیة» On the Day of Judgment, they will be questioned about prayer.

12- The establishment of prayer is for His remembrance, «أقم الصّلوة لذكرى», and His remembrance causes tranquility in hearts. «الا بذكر اللّه تَطمئنّ القلوب» (28). Just as our remembrance of God causes God's remembrance of us. «اُذكرونى اَذكركم» (Remember Me, I will remember you) (29).

13- Monotheism (Tawhid) and the Resurrection (Ma'ad) are the first Divine messages for humanity. «لااله الاّانَا، السّاعة آتیة»

14- No one except Allah is aware of the time the Day of Judgment will occur. «اَكاد اُخفیها»

15- The establishment of the Day of Judgment is certain, «انّ الساعة آتیة», but its time is hidden. «اُخفیها»

16- Not every piece of knowledge or awareness is beneficial. «اَكاد اُخفیها» Not knowing the time of the Day of Judgment contains wisdom and grace. This is because man remains free in his actions and is not afflicted by panic or fear. A person who does not know the hour of a guest's arrival constantly maintains his readiness.

17- It is through the concealment of the time of the Day of Judgment that people's reward will be based on realities and commensurate with their effort and striving. «اُخفیها... لتجزى»

18- Worldly reward and punishment are not complete. «اِنّ الساعة... لتجزى»

19- On the Day of Judgment, the implementation of law, reward, and punishment is equal for everyone. «كلّ نفس»

20- Reward is based on effort and striving, whether it reaches completion or not. «لتجزى - بماتسعى»

(28) Ra'd, 28.

(29) Baqarah, 152.

Ayah

فَلَا يَصُدَّنَّكَ عَنْهَا مَن لَّا يُؤْمِنُ بِهَا وَٱتَّبَعَ هَوَىٰهُ فَتَرْدَىٰ (۱٦)
So do not let yourself be distracted from it by those who do not believe in it and who follow their desires, or you will perish.’ (16)

Messages

1- Mere belief in and practice of the fundamentals and branches (of religion) is not enough; rather, firmness against opponents is also necessary. «لااله الا انا - فاعبدنى فلا یصدّنّك»

2- Disbelievers seek to turn you away from the path of truth. Their plotting and obstruction should not cause any disruption in your way and method. «فلایصدّنّك»

3- The source of denying the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is following carnal desires (hawa al-nafs). (30) «مَن لایؤمن بها واتّبع هویه»

4- Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the best factor for controlling desire-worship. «مَن لایؤمن بها و اتّبع هویه»

5- The result of desire-worship is downfall, even if that person is a Prophet. «فتردى»

6- Compromise with disbelievers (kuffar) and faithless individuals leads to destruction. «فلا یصدّنّك... فتردى»

7- Disbelievers and desire-worshippers seek to prevent believers from performing prayer (Salat). «أقِم الصّلوة... لایصدّنّك عنها»

(30) Perhaps the reason why the lack of faith is expressed using the present tense phrase «لا یؤمن» (la yu'min), and the following of desires is expressed using the past tense phrase «واتّبع هویه» (wa-ittaba'a hawahu), is that the failure to believe stems from the following of desires. Tafsir al-Mizan.

Ayahs

وَمَا تِلْكَ بِيَمِينِكَ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ (۱۷)
‘Moses, what is that in your right hand?’ (17)
قَالَ هِىَ عَصَاىَ أَتَوَكَّؤُا۟ عَلَيْهَا وَأَهُشُّ بِهَا عَلَىٰ غَنَمِى وَلِىَ فِيهَا مَـَٔارِبُ أُخْرَىٰ (۱۸)
He said, ‘It is my staff. I lean on it, and with it I beat down leaves for my sheep; and I have other uses for it.’ (18)
قَالَ أَلْقِهَا يَـٰمُوسَىٰ (۱۹)
He said, ‘Moses, throw it down.’ (19)
فَأَلْقَىٰهَا فَإِذَا هِىَ حَيَّةٌ تَسْعَىٰ (۲٠)
So he threw it down, and behold, it was a snake, moving swiftly. (20)
قَالَ خُذْهَا وَلَا تَخَفْ ۖ سَنُعِيدُهَا سِيرَتَهَا ٱلْأُولَىٰ (۲۱)
He said, ‘Take hold of it, and do not fear. We will restore it to its former state. (21)

Key Points

Humans naturally fear danger and factors of evil and harm, and they avoid confronting them. It is obvious that Moses (عليه السلام), being human, would also fear. What is subject to criticism and blame is being affected, fearing, and having anxiety in the heart due to other than Allah, which is contrary to courage and is among the base qualities. And certainly, Moses was far from that. Perhaps the fear of Moses (عليه السلام) was because He thought the command "القها" (Cast it down) was for reverence, like the command "فاخلع نعلیك" (Remove your sandals), but as soon as it turned into a serpent, He was startled. In fact, this very fear is itself proof that this event was a miracle. Otherwise, a sorcerer who knows his magic is not real does not fear his own sorcery and magic. Allah gives life to millions of inanimate objects every moment, or takes the lives of millions of living creatures. Here too, He gave life to the inanimate staff, then took it back, and it became a staff once more. (31) Can He not cause a living human to die and then restore him to his original form? Truly, if the Divine gaze turns wood into a dragon, then what will happen if He casts a gaze of favor upon us?! And if His gaze swallows the magic of all sorcerers, will His gaze upon our hearts not swallow all temptations?! Right at the beginning of this mission, two miracles occurred for Moses: one was that the staff turned into a dragon, "فاذا هى حَیّة تَسعى" (and behold, it was a serpent, moving rapidly), and the other was that the dragon returned to its original form as a staff. "سیرتها الاولى" (its former state).

31) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Quran.

Messages

1- To display His power, Allah uses even the closest and simplest tools. "و ما تلك بیمینك یا موسى"

2- By utilizing multi-purpose tools, one can gain greater benefit from a smaller volume. "اَتَوكّؤ علیها واهُشّ بها ..."

3- Relying on a staff and material tools does not contradict reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul). "اتوكّؤ علیها"

4- Shepherding was the occupation of most Prophets. "اهُشّ بها على غنمى"

5- Practice, experience, and preparation are necessary before the call (to prophethood). Moses (عليه السلام), who was about to cast down the staff before Pharaoh so that it would become a dragon, needed to have witnessed the scene Himself beforehand. "فالقاها فاذا هى حَیّة تسعى"

Ayahs

وَٱضْمُمْ يَدَكَ إِلَىٰ جَنَاحِكَ تَخْرُجْ بَيْضَآءَ مِنْ غَيْرِ سُوٓءٍ ءَايَةً أُخْرَىٰ (۲۲)
Now clasp your hand to your armpit: it will emerge white, without any harm—[this is yet] another sign, (22)
لِنُرِيَكَ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا ٱلْكُبْرَى (۲۳)
that We may show you some of Our great signs. (23)
ٱذْهَبْ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّهُۥ طَغَىٰ (۲٤)
Go to Pharaoh. He has indeed rebelled.’ (24)

Key Points

The second miracle of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) was the "Yad al-Bayda" (the White Hand), which is mentioned in the Quran with three expressions: one is «اُسلك یدك فى جیبك» (32), another is «وادخل یدك فى جیبك» (33), and the third is the Ayah «اُضمُم یدك الى جناحك». It is possible to reconcile all of them and say: Put your hand into your bosom and extend it up to your armpit. In the Torah, it is stated that the whiteness of Moses' hand was due to the disease of leprosy or vitiligo (34). However, the Quran uses the phrase «من غیر سوء» (without harm/evil) to indicate that the whiteness of the hand was light, not a disease, and that the Torah has been distorted (tahrif).

32) Qasas, 32. 33) Naml, 12. 34) Torah, Book of Exodus, Chapter 4, Verse 6; Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- The Hand of Divine Power is open, and He can manifest His power at any moment, by any means He wills. «اُضمُم یدك»

2- The greater the arrogance and obstinacy of the audience, the more miracles must be presented. «آیة اُخرى» (For Pharaohs, several miracles are necessary.)

3- Whoever intends to invite the tyrant (Taghut) to the truth must first have witnessed the signs of the truth himself. «لنُرِیَك من آیاتنا الكُبرى»

4- The miracles of the Prophets are only a part of the Divine signs. «من آیاتنا»

5- The first mission of the Prophets is to struggle against the tyrants (Taghuts). «اِذهَب»

6- One should not always wait for the people to come; sometimes it is necessary for even the Prophets to move toward the rulers for their guidance. «اِذهب الى فرعون اِنّه طَغى»

7- To reform society, one must go to the source and root of sedition and corruption. «اِذهب الى فرعون»

8- Religion is not separate from politics; Moses moved to reform the government. «اِذهب الى فرعون»

9- Struggling against the tyrant (Taghut) is a Divine duty, not a political show. «اِذهَب»

10- The source of all denials is rebellion (tughyan) and arrogance against the truth. «اِنّه طَغى» Pharaoh's rebellion manifested in claiming divinity, creating terror, causing division, and enslaving the people.

11- Only those who have built themselves up through faith, worship, and steadfastness can struggle against the tyrant (Taghut). «فاعبدنى... أقم الصّلوة... لایصدّنّك...اِذهَب الى...»

Ayahs

قَالَ رَبِّ ٱشْرَحْ لِى صَدْرِى (۲٥)
He said, ‘My Lord! Open my breast for me. (25)
وَيَسِّرْ لِىٓ أَمْرِى (۲٦)
Make my task easy for me. (26)
وَٱحْلُلْ عُقْدَةً مِّن لِّسَانِى (۲۷)
Remove the hitch from my tongue, (27)
يَفْقَهُوا۟ قَوْلِى (۲۸)
[so that] they may understand my speech. (28)

Key Points

As soon as Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) was commissioned with the Divine mission (Risalah), he requested four things from Allah: 1- Broadness of chest (patience/tolerance). «ربّ اشرح لى صدرى» 2- Favorable conditions. «و یَسّرلى أمرى» 3- Fluent speech. «واحلُل عُقدةً من لسانى» 4- Having a minister (helper). «واجعل لى وزیراً»

Since Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) was commissioned with numerous issues—devotional, political, revolutionary, social, ethical, individual, and collective—and naturally, focusing the necessary attention on each one distracts a person from other matters, therefore, broadness of chest (patience) is absolutely essential for achieving all these goals.(35) Broadness of chest is necessary both for the individual himself and for carrying out the responsibility. The word "لى" (for me) symbolizes that receiving revelation requires great capacity. Just as it is mentioned regarding the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) in Surah Al-Inshirah: «ألم نَشرَح لك صدرك... اِنّ مع العُسر یُسرا»

Heart of the Believer Heart of the Disbeliever
1- Life. «أو مَن كان میتاً فاحییناه»(36) 1- Sickness. «فى قلوبهم مَرَض»(37)

Messages

1- Broadness of chest is the first condition for success in any endeavor. «ربّ اشرح لى صدرى»

2- Instead of fearing and fleeing responsibility, we should ask Allah for the means, prerequisites, and tools needed for it. «ربّ اشرح لى صدرى»

3- Supplication before any task is the symbol of reliance (Tawakkul) and a means of repelling dangers and afflictions along the way, even though supplication itself is considered worship and a declaration of need. «ربّ اشرح لى صدرى»

4- Guiding and educating people and fighting against tyrants (Taghuts) is entirely dependent on the patience and tolerance of the educator and the mujahid (striver). «ربّ اشرح لى صدرى»

5- The prophetic mission is incompatible with harshness and violence. A great task requires a great spirit. «ربّ اشرح لى صدرى» (Moses (عليه السلام) had killed a person with a punch in a conflict, which is why he requested broadness of chest from Allah.)

6- The supplications of the Prophets in the Quran begin with the Name «ربّ» (Lord). «ربّ»

7- Receiving Divine favors requires a broad spirit and chest. «ربّ اشرح لى صدرى»

8- With broadness of chest, tasks become easy. «ربّ اشرح لى صدرى و یسّر لى أمرى»

9- Simple speech and fluent expression are perfection. «واحلُل عُقدةً من لسانى»

10- In guiding and mobilizing people, boosting the morale of forces, and intimidating and weakening the morale of the tyrant, eloquent speech is very important. «واحلُل عُقدة من لسانى»

11- The fundamental goal is for people to understand, not [the use of] beautiful terminology. «یَفقَهوا قولى» The condition for success in propagation (Tabligh) is that people clearly understand the words of the preacher.

13- In propagation, every means that deepens people's understanding must be utilized. «یَفقَهوا قولى»

Ayahs

وَٱجْعَل لِّى وَزِيرًا مِّنْ أَهْلِى (۲۹)
Appoint for me a minister from my family, (29)
هَـٰرُونَ أَخِى (۳٠)
Aaron, my brother. (30)
ٱشْدُدْ بِهِۦٓ أَزْرِى (۳۱)
Strengthen my back through him, (31)
وَأَشْرِكْهُ فِىٓ أَمْرِى (۳۲)
and make him my associate in my task, (32)
كَىْ نُسَبِّحَكَ كَثِيرًا (۳۳)
so that we may glorify You greatly, (33)
وَنَذْكُرَكَ كَثِيرًا (۳٤)
and remember You greatly. (34)

Key Points

The term Wazīr (وزیر) derives from Wizr (وِزْر), meaning a heavy burden, and refers to someone who carries the burden of another's responsibility as well. The word azrī (اَزْرى) also means "my back/support." It is mentioned in the commentaries (tafāsīr) that the noble Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله recited these verses many times and said: O Allah, I also desire a minister (wazīr) from my own family, just like Moses عليه السلام requested. (54) Prophet Moses عليه السلام had no difficulty in receiving revelation, and his request for a minister was solely due to the heaviness of the responsibility of the prophethood (risālah) and the difficulty of propagation (tablīgh). Aaron عليه السلام was three years older than Moses, and he passed away earlier than Moses عليه السلام by the same margin. (55) And all subsequent prophets were descendants of Aaron عليه السلام. Allamah Tabataba'i states in Tafsīr al-Mīzān: The Hadith of Manzilah, which the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله addressed to Ali عليه السلام, stating: «انت منّى بمنزلة هارون من موسى الاّ أنّه لانبىّ بعدى» (You are to me in the position of Aaron to Moses, except that there is no prophet after me), has been narrated through one hundred chains of transmission by the Sunnis and seventy chains by the Shi'a.

Seeking assistance from people does not contradict reliance (tawakkul) upon Allah and seeking His help, nor is it polytheism (shirk). The issue of seeking assistance and cooperation has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran, including: «تعاونوا على البِرّ و التّقوى» (Cooperate in righteousness and piety) (56). Just as Prophet Jesus said: «مَن انصارى الى اللّه» (Who are my helpers toward Allah?) (57). Or when Allah replied to the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: «حسبك اللّه و مَن اتّبعك من المؤمنین» (Sufficient for you is Allah and those who follow you among the believers) (58). Or when Dhul-Qarnayn requested help from the people to build the barrier and said: «اعینونى بقوّة...» (Assist me with strength...) (59). And in this incident, too, Prophet Moses sought a worthy person from Allah to help him in the propagation of the religion.

Messages

1- Fighting a tyrant like Pharaoh is not possible alone. «اِجعل لى وزیراً»

2- The more important the task, the more critical the selection of the minister and companion becomes. The minister proposed by Moses عليه السلام must be confirmed by Allah. «اِجعل لى وزیراً»

3- It is better if one's collaborator is from his own family. (60) «من اهلى»

4- Leadership and management must be concentrated in a single person, and others should cooperate with him. «اُشدُد به أزرى، أشركهُ فى أمرى»

5- Cooperation leads to the advancement of affairs, provided the collaborator is like-minded and supportive, not opposed. «أشركه فى أمرى»

6- True glorification (tasbīh) occurs in the context of fighting tyranny. «اِنّه طَغى ، كى نسبّحك»

7- The philosophy of governance and power is the establishment and elevation of spirituality in society. «اُشدُد به أزرى كى نسبّحك كثیراً و نذكرك كثیرا»

8- The duty of the prophets is to purify Allah from polytheism (shirk), delusions, and superstitions. «نسبّحك»

9- Great responsibility requires great glorification. «نُسبّحك، كثیراً»

10- First, we should purify Allah from every fault and imperfection, and then engage in His remembrance (dhikr) and gratitude (shukr). «نسبّحك، نذكرك»

11- What is valuable is frequent remembrance (dhikr kathīr); otherwise, every human remembers Allah during danger and distress. «نذكرك كثیراً»

60) Provided, of course, that he possesses the necessary merit and competence, just as Prophet Moses considered Aaron's eloquence (fasāhah) and said: «هو افصح منّى» (He is more eloquent than me).

Ayahs

إِنَّكَ كُنتَ بِنَا بَصِيرًا (۳٥)
Indeed You see us best.’ (35)
قَالَ قَدْ أُوتِيتَ سُؤْلَكَ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ (۳٦)
He said, ‘Moses, your request has been granted! (36)
وَلَقَدْ مَنَنَّا عَلَيْكَ مَرَّةً أُخْرَىٰٓ (۳۷)
Certainly, We have done you a favour another time, (37)

Messages

1- The Prophets always saw themselves in the Divine presence, and were content with His will and wisdom. «اِنّك كنتَ بنا بصیراً»

2- Although Allah is aware of everything, prayer (du'a) and requesting from Him are still desirable. «ربّ اشرح... اِنّك كنتَ بنا بصیراً»

3- If the request is correct and appropriate, and the prayer is sincere, the response (acceptance) is certain. «قد اوتیت»

4- Divine bestowal is wise and based on insight (or clear vision). «بنا بصیراً... اوتیت سؤلك»

5- When We appoint someone and commission him to perform a task, We should also provide the tools and fulfill his needs. «اِذهَب الى فرعون... ربّ اشرح لى صدرى... وزیراً من اهلى... قد اوتیت»

6- Divine blessings are granted out of favor (or grace), not entitlement. «و لقد مننّا»

7- Never forget the favors of Allah. «و لقد مننّا علیك مَرّة اُخرى»

Ayahs

إِذْ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّكَ مَا يُوحَىٰٓ (۳۸)
when We revealed to your mother whatever was to be revealed: (38)
أَنِ ٱقْذِفِيهِ فِى ٱلتَّابُوتِ فَٱقْذِفِيهِ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ فَلْيُلْقِهِ ٱلْيَمُّ بِٱلسَّاحِلِ يَأْخُذْهُ عَدُوٌّ لِّى وَعَدُوٌّ لَّهُۥ ۚ وَأَلْقَيْتُ عَلَيْكَ مَحَبَّةً مِّنِّى وَلِتُصْنَعَ عَلَىٰ عَيْنِىٓ (۳۹)
“Put him in the casket, and cast it into the river. Then the river will cast it on the bank, and he shall be picked up by an enemy of Mine and an enemy of his.” And I made you endearing, and that you might be reared under My [watchful] eyes. (39)

Key Points

The meaning of wahy (revelation) here is inspiration (ilham) and suggestion (ilqa) upon the heart, not the technical wahy specific to the Prophets. This is because prophethood (messengership) is exclusive to men. As stated in Surah Yusuf, Ayah 109: «و ما ارسلنا من قبلك الاّ رجالاً نُوحى الیهم»

«تابوت» (61) means chest or ark, and «یم» means sea or river. All eight instances of the use of «یم» in the Quran occur in the story of Prophet Moses.

Contrary to today, where san'at (industry/craft) is only applied to the manufacturing of goods, the Quran applies san'at both to goods (as in «اِصنَعِ الفلك» - "Construct the Ark") and to human upbringing (as in «لتصنع على عینى» - "that you might be brought up under My eye").

As soon as Allah wills a matter, He provides all its prerequisites. Here, to protect Moses عليه السلام, He inspires his mother to cast her child into the river, commands the river to carry the chest holding the child to the shore, suggests to the heart of the enemy that he should take him, and places the love of the child in the enemy's heart.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Allah placed such love for Moses in the hearts of the people that whoever saw Moses عليه السلام became attached to him. (62)

The secret to achieving perfection is detachment from dependencies and affections. «اِقذِفیه» (Cast him [into the river]).

A: Detachment from self; Abraham was ready to fall into the fire.

B: Detachment from spouse; Abraham left his wife in waterless and barren Mecca.

C: Detachment from the infant; Moses' mother cast her child into the water.

D: Detachment from the youth; Abraham was ready to sacrifice Ishmael.

E: Detachment from lust; Joseph refused the request for gratification from the wife of the Aziz of Egypt.

F: Detachment from sleep; no one but Allah knows the reward for prayer in the middle of the night. «فلا تَعلم نفس ما اُخفى لهم من قرّة اعین» (63)

(61) The Tabut (Ark) referred to in the story of Talut and Goliath in Ayah 248 of Surah Al-Baqarah is this very chest of Prophet Moses. «... ان یأتیكم التابوت فیه سكینة من ربّكم وبقیّة ممّا ترك آل موسى وآل هارون»

(62) Tafsir Namuneh.

(63) Sajdah, 17.

Messages

1- Sometimes parents receive special divine favor because of their child. «اوحینا الى امّك»

2- A woman can also reach a status where she is subject to divine revelation and inspiration. «اوحینا الى اُمّك» (Although this revelation is not considered prophethood/messengership, it is also not considered simple inspiration, because through it, Moses' mother entrusted her child to the waves of the water.)

3- Women have also played a role in the movement and mission of the Prophets. «امّك» (Your mother).

4- The sea, like all of existence, is under the command of Allah. «فلیلقه الیمّ بالساحل» (Then let the river cast him onto the shore).

5- The repetition of the word «عدّو» (enemy) indicates the depth of the hostility of the tyrant (Pharaoh) towards the messengers of Allah. «عدوٌّ لى و عدوٌّ له» (an enemy to Me and an enemy to him).

6- An enemy can become a source of good, if Allah wills it. «یأخذه عدوٌّ لى و عدوٌّ له» (He will be picked up by an enemy to Me and an enemy to him).

7- Popularity (or being loved) is one of Allah's favors. He to Whom hearts belong is also the source of love. «محبّة منّى» (love from Me).

8- The friends of Allah (Awliya) are under the special supervision and upbringing of the Lord. «على عینى» (under My eye).

Ayah

إِذْ تَمْشِىٓ أُخْتُكَ فَتَقُولُ هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ مَن يَكْفُلُهُۥ ۖ فَرَجَعْنَـٰكَ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّكَ كَىْ تَقَرَّ عَيْنُهَا وَلَا تَحْزَنَ ۚ وَقَتَلْتَ نَفْسًا فَنَجَّيْنَـٰكَ مِنَ ٱلْغَمِّ وَفَتَنَّـٰكَ فُتُونًا ۚ فَلَبِثْتَ سِنِينَ فِىٓ أَهْلِ مَدْيَنَ ثُمَّ جِئْتَ عَلَىٰ قَدَرٍ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ (٤٠)
When your sister walked up [to Pharaoh’s palace] saying, “Shall I show you someone who will take care of him?” Then We restored you to your mother, that she might not grieve and be comforted. Then you slew a soul, whereupon We delivered you from anguish, and We tried you with various ordeals. Then you stayed for several years among the people of Midian. Then you turned up as ordained, O Moses! (40)

Key Points

When Pharaoh retrieved the chest from the water, he initially intended to kill the infant, but his wife restrained him from doing so. Therefore, they invited wet nurses to suckle the child, but Moses refused the breast of every one of them, and the Pharaonic system failed in nourishing the child. Moses' sister, who was following the events, came forward and, in a casual manner, said: I can introduce you to someone whose breast this child will accept. In this way, Moses returned to his mother's care.

In the history of the life and struggles of Prophet Moses عليه السلام, several women played essential roles: Moses' mother, Moses' sister, Moses' wife, and Pharaoh's wife.

The events in the lives of the Prophets sometimes resemble one another. For instance, the story of Prophet Moses عليه السلام is similar to the life of Prophet Joseph عليه السلام in several aspects, including: A: Both Prophets lived in a palace for a period. B: Living in the palace was not the choice of either of them. C: The parents of both Prophets suffered separation. D: Ultimately, both returned to their parents. E: Both were afflicted by the trials (fitnah) of others; Prophet Joseph by the trial of his brothers, and Moses عليه السلام by the trial of Pharaoh. F: In the life of each, a palace-dwelling woman played a role: the wife of the Aziz (Governor) of Egypt in a negative role, and the wife of Pharaoh in a positive role.

Messages

1- In a tyrannical regime, every supporter of the truth, whether male or female, must fulfill their role in the struggle effectively. «تَمشى اُختك»

2- To receive divine favors, one must take action. «اوحینا... تَمشى اُختك»

3- We should present our rightful and logical suggestions gently and kindly. «هل»

4- We should not reveal secrets. «مَن یكفله» and she did not say: «الى امّه»

5- Breastfeeding a child is not enough; rather, complete guardianship (takafful) is important. «یكفله»

6- Allah is faithful to His promises.(64) «اِنّا رادّوه الیك - فرجعناك الى اُمّك»

7- A mother's guardianship provides the best conditions for a child's growth. «لتصنع على عینى... فرجعناك الى اُمّك»

8- A mother's satisfaction holds a special status with Allah. «فرجعناك الى اُمّك كى تقرّ عینها»

9- Worrying about the repercussions of one's actions does not harm sincerity (ikhlas) or infallibility ('ismah). «فنجّیناك من الغمّ» (Moses عليه السلام had killed a Copt unintentionally, and although he had committed no sin, he was worried and distressed.)

10- Men of God face severe trials. «فتنّاك فتوناً» Prophets must also taste the bitterness of difficult events; thus, after years of living in the palace, shepherding in the desert now begins. «فلبثت سنین فى اهل مدین»

11- Overcoming difficult stages is a prerequisite for attaining divine and spiritual status. «فتنّاك... ثمّ جئت على قدر یا موسى»

12- Responsibilities, divine successes, and favors are timed and calculated. «جئت على قدر یا موسى»

64) Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The period of separation between the child and his mother was three days. Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Ayahs

وَٱصْطَنَعْتُكَ لِنَفْسِى (٤۱)
And I chose you for Myself.’ (41)
ٱذْهَبْ أَنتَ وَأَخُوكَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِى وَلَا تَنِيَا فِى ذِكْرِى (٤۲)
‘Go ahead, you and your brother, with My signs and do not flag in My remembrance. (42)
ٱذْهَبَآ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّهُۥ طَغَىٰ (٤۳)
Both of you go to Pharaoh, for he has indeed rebelled. (43)
فَقُولَا لَهُۥ قَوْلًا لَّيِّنًا لَّعَلَّهُۥ يَتَذَكَّرُ أَوْ يَخْشَىٰ (٤٤)
Speak to him in a soft manner; maybe he will take admonition or fear.’ (44)

Key Points

Khashyah (reverential fear) is a fear arising from knowledge and a feeling of awe in the presence of another. Allah has stated specific descriptions regarding Prophet Musa (Moses) علیه السلام: A: «قرّبناه نجیّا» (65) We brought him close for intimate converse. B: «انَا اختَرتُك» (66) I have chosen you. C: «ولتصنع على عینى» (67) So that you may be trained under My eye. D: «اِصتَنعتك لنفسى» I fashioned you for Myself.

In the previous verses, two miracles were mentioned for Musa علیه السلام: the transformation of the staff into a serpent and the White Hand. In this verse, He says: «بآیاتى», meaning We will also grant you other miracles.

In verse 24 of this very Surah, the command came: «اِذهَب الى فرعون» (Go forth to Pharaoh). However, Musa علیه السلام requested a helper from Allah. Now, with the granting of his request, this verse states: «اِذهَبا الى فرعون» (Go forth, both of you, to Pharaoh).

Adi ibn Hatim narrates: When Amir al-Mu'minin علیه السلام encountered Mu'awiyah at Siffin, He declared loudly: "I swear by Allah, I will fight Mu'awiyah and his companions," but immediately followed it by saying softly: Insha'Allah (If Allah wills). I said: "O Ali! You speak loudly about fighting, but you say Insha'Allah softly?" He replied: "War is deception, and I am not a liar. My purpose in doing this is to incite my companions to fight so that they do not disperse." Then the Imam cited this verse as evidence for His action, stating that Allah told Musa: «فقولا... لعلّه یتذكّر» (Speak... perhaps he may take heed). Allah knew that Pharaoh would not take heed, but He spoke in this manner to encourage Musa to go toward Pharaoh. (68)

(65) Maryam, 52. (66) Taha, 13. (67) Taha, 39. (68) Kafi, Vol. 7, p. 460

Messages

1- Individuals must be trained for great objectives. «واصطنعتك لنفسى» 2- Allah has specific plans for the Prophets. «واصطنعتك لنفسى» 3- Self-building first, then community-building. «اصطنعتك... اذهب» 4- If, as a leader, we dispatch someone for a task, we must strive to ensure their morale is filled with love, a sense of pride, and encouragement. «اصطنعتك لنفسى... اِذهَب» 5- Sometimes others must be summoned, «و أتونى مسلمین» (69) and sometimes one must go toward them oneself. «اِذهَب» 6- In some stages, the messengers and bearers of the message should not be a single person. «اِذهب أنتَ و أخوك» 7- The preacher must be equipped with the tools of guidance. «بآیاتى» 8- The preacher must be brave, determined, and impenetrable. «ولا تَنیا فى ذكرى» (Do not slacken in My remembrance), lest the palace, power, and awe of the tyrant intimidate him in carrying out his mission, making him neglect the remembrance of Allah and abandon the necessary clarity. 9- The secret to human success is complete and constant attention to Allah. «لاتَنیا فى ذكرى» 10- Preaching, guidance, enjoining good and forbidding evil, are all remembrance and recollection of Allah. «اِذهَب انتَ و أخوك بایاتى ولا تَنیا فى ذكرى» 11- One of the greatest goals and services of the Prophets is fighting tyranny (taghut) and removing obstacles from the path of monotheism. «اِذهَب اِنّه طَغى » 12- The first step in enjoining good and forbidding evil is gentle speech. «فقولا له قولاً لیّنا» 13- Hopes lie in gentleness, and nothing is expected from harshness. « قولاً لیّنا لعّله یتذكّر...» 14- The beginning of training and guidance must be through sincere dialogue. (70) «فقولاله قولاً لیّنا» Even with the most rebellious individuals, one must first speak with softness and kindness. 15- Never despair of guiding others. «لعّله یتذكّر ...» 16- The duty of the Prophet is to remind and guide. «یتذكّر او یخشى»

(69) Naml, 31. (70) Allah also tells the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: Deal with the people with softness and kindness. «فبما رحمة من اللّه لنت لهم ولو كنت فظاً غلیظ القلب لانفضوا من حولك» (It is by the mercy of Allah that you were lenient with them. Had you been harsh and hard-hearted, they would have dispersed from around you.) Al Imran, 159.

Ayahs

قَالَا رَبَّنَآ إِنَّنَا نَخَافُ أَن يَفْرُطَ عَلَيْنَآ أَوْ أَن يَطْغَىٰ (٤٥)
They said, ‘Our Lord! We are indeed afraid that he will forestall us or will exceed all bounds.’ (45)
قَالَ لَا تَخَافَآ ۖ إِنَّنِى مَعَكُمَآ أَسْمَعُ وَأَرَىٰ (٤٦)
He said, ‘Do not be afraid, for I will be with the two of you, hearing and seeing [whatever happens]. (46)

Key Points

The word farṭ (فَرط) means to speed up and get ahead. And an yafruta ‘alaynā (أن یَفرُط علینا) means we fear that Pharaoh might preempt us before we present the argument and bring the miracles, thus increasing his tyranny against the Children of Israel. (71) What is blameworthy for the Prophets is inner fear (khashyah) and dread of anything other than Allah. Otherwise, natural fear (khawf ṭabī‘ī) is a necessary component of the humanity of those great figures and all human beings, and every person experiences this fear when facing dangers.

71) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

1- Examining potential problems and obstacles, and fearing the future or failure, does not contradict sincerity (ikhlāṣ) or the station of prophethood. Innā nakhāfu... (اننّا نخاف...)

2- Creating a hostile atmosphere and spreading rumors is the method of tyrants (ṭāghūt) and oppressors. They poison the environment, accuse the men of God, or resort to chaos after hearing the truth so that the righteous word does not take effect. Yafruta ‘alaynā aw an yaṭghā (یَفرُط علینا او أن یَطغى).

3- Faith in being present before Allah and certainty in Divine assistance are factors that bring courage and boost the morale of the believers. Innani ma‘akumā (اِنّنى معكما).

4- If you commission someone for a task, you must provide for them in every respect and boost their morale. Innani ma‘akumā asma‘u wa arā (اِنّنى معكما أسمع و أرى).

5- Although all human beings are subject to Divine attention and mercy, His favor upon the Prophets is greater. Innani ma‘akumā asma‘u wa arā (اِنّنى معكما أسمع و أرى).

Ayahs

فَأْتِيَاهُ فَقُولَآ إِنَّا رَسُولَا رَبِّكَ فَأَرْسِلْ مَعَنَا بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَلَا تُعَذِّبْهُمْ ۖ قَدْ جِئْنَـٰكَ بِـَٔايَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكَ ۖ وَٱلسَّلَـٰمُ عَلَىٰ مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ ٱلْهُدَىٰٓ (٤۷)
So approach him and say, ‘‘We are the apostles of your Lord. Let the Children of Israel go with us, and do not torture them! We certainly bring you a sign from your Lord, and may peace be upon him who follows guidance! (47)
إِنَّا قَدْ أُوحِىَ إِلَيْنَآ أَنَّ ٱلْعَذَابَ عَلَىٰ مَن كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ (٤۸)
Indeed it has been revealed to us that punishment shall befall those who impugn us and turn their backs [on us].’’ ’ (48)

Key Points

Since guiding and preaching to the tyrannical leaders and leading them was a very difficult and important task, Allah therefore repeats the command to move towards Pharaoh: «اِذهَب الى فرعون» (Go to Pharaoh), «اِذهَب أنتَ و أخوك» (Go, you and your brother), «اِذهَبا الى فرعون» (Go, both of you, to Pharaoh), «فأتیاه» (Then go to him). In these verses, three pillars of belief are mentioned: Tawhid (Monotheism), Prophethood, and the Resurrection (Ma'ad): «ربّك» (Your Lord), «اُوحى الینا» (It has been revealed to us), «أنّ العذاب» (That the punishment...). Allah structured the instructions for the mission of Moses and Aaron علیهما السلام in five stages: A: Going. «فأتیاه» B: Speaking. «فقولا» C: Presenting evidence. «جئناك بأیة» D: Encouragement. «والسّلام على مَنِ اتّبع الهُدى» E: Threatening. «أنّ العذاب على مَن كَذّب و تَولّى».

Messages

1- Guidance and missionary work must be innovative and offensive (proactive), not defensive. «فأتیاه»

2- The mission of the prophets is among the affairs of Allah’s Lordship (Rububiyyah). «رسولا ربّك»

3- Break the arrogance of the oppressors. «ربّك» (Your Lord) stands against the one who says: «انا ربّكم الاعلى» (I am your Lord, the Most High) (72).

4- Speaking with gentleness and kindness does not contradict decisiveness and clarity. «انا رسولا ربّك»

5- Liberating people from the oppression and servitude of the tyrant (Taghut) is at the forefront of the prophets' duties. «ارسل معنا بنى اسرائیل» (Yes, religion is not separate from politics; struggling against tyrants and freeing the people is central to the divine commands.)

6- Migration (Hijrah) from an environment of oppression and sin is the prelude to salvation. First freedom, then faith. «ارسل معنا بنى اسرائیل...»

7- The struggle against the tyrant and the liberation of the people only possess true and comprehensive value when they are aligned with the path of the prophets. «ارسل معنا»

8- Harming and torturing people is forbidden. «لا تعذّبهم»

9- The actions of subordinates are also accounted for by their commander and superior. «لاتعذّبهم» (Do not torment them), considering that Pharaoh himself was not the one torturing every single person.

10- Complete and comprehensive security and well-being are achieved only through following divine guidance. «والسلام على مَن اتّبع الهُدى»

11- Greeting (giving Salam to) the tyrant, the oppressors, and those who are not on the path of guidance is forbidden. «والسلام على مَن اتّبع الهُدى»

12- What is important is following the truth. «اِتّبع الهُدى»

13- Giving Salam at the end of a speech is among the etiquette of discourse. «والسلام على...»

(72) An-Nazi'at, 24.

Ayahs

قَالَ فَمَن رَّبُّكُمَا يَـٰمُوسَىٰ (٤۹)
He said, ‘Who is your Lord, Moses?’ (49)
قَالَ رَبُّنَا ٱلَّذِىٓ أَعْطَىٰ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ خَلْقَهُۥ ثُمَّ هَدَىٰ (٥٠)
He said, ‘Our Lord is He who gave everything its creation and then guided it.’ (50)
قَالَ فَمَا بَالُ ٱلْقُرُونِ ٱلْأُولَىٰ (٥۱)
He said, ‘What about the former generations?’ (51)

Key Points

"Bāl" (بال) means thought, but it is also used to mean state (or condition). What was at issue for Pharaoh was Lordship (Rubūbiyyah), not Divinity (Ulūhiyyah) or Creatorship (Khāliqiyyah), because he did not claim Creatorship, and he himself had deities: «و یَذَرك و الهتك»(73). Other disbelievers also accepted "Allah" as the Creator: «و لئن سألتهم مَن خَلق السّموات و الارض... لیقولُنّ اللّه»(74). Rather, Pharaoh's claim was his own absolute leadership and sovereignty. Therefore, Moses عليه السلام replied: The Creator, the Administrator, and the Ruler of existence is one. All the Prophets invited people to Monotheism (Tawhīd) and considered the only path to perfection and guidance to be from Allah. Prophet Abraham عليه السلام said: «الّذى خلَقنى فهو یَهدین»(75), the God Who created me, He guides me. Moses عليه السلام said: «أعطى كلّ شى ء خلقه ثمّ هَدى»(76), He gave everything its creation, then guided it. And Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله said: «الّذى خلق فَسَوّى , و الّذى قدّر فهَدى»(77), the God Who created, then proportioned, and Who measured, then guided. The leadership of the movement and the uprising belonged to Moses عليه السلام. «یا موسى» (O Moses). Although Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh, and he initially said: «مَن ربّكما» (Who is your Lord?), Moses was the leader, so Pharaoh immediately added: «یا موسى».

73) Al-A'raf, 127. 74) Al-'Ankabut, 61. 75) Taha, 50. 76) Ash-Shu'ara, 78. 77) Al-A'la, 2 - 3.

Messages

1- What is claimed and disputed is the Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) of Allah, not Creatorship. «مَن ربّكما»

2- The spirit of arrogance and tyrannical pride is evident even in the speech and manner of questioning. Instead of asking, "Who is my Lord?" he said: «مَن ربّكما» (Who is your Lord?).

3- We have no claim upon Allah; everything that exists is His gift. «أعطى كلّ شى ء»

4- The One God both creates and administers through inherent (takwīnī) guidance. «أعطى كلّ شى ء خلقه ثمّ هَدى » (Yes, after creation, He also placed within them the path for the continuation of life and perfection. «ثمّ هدى»)

5- Allah has bestowed upon every creature a complete creation and characteristics appropriate to it. «خلقه»

6- The necessity of Divine Lordship is His guidance. «ربّنا... ثمّ هدى»

7- Lordship is the right of the One Who is the Creator. «ربّنا الّذى اعطى كلّ شى ء خلقه»

8- All creatures are subject to Divine guidance. All the laws governing existence and the instincts present in humans and animals are from Divine guidance. «خلقه ثمّ هدى»

9- In the face of logic, one should not rely on the path of the ancestors. «فما بال القرون الاولى» Moses عليه السلام speaks with logic, but Pharaoh shifts the discussion toward history to incite the emotions of the people. «فما بال القرون الاولى»

Ayah

قَالَ عِلْمُهَا عِندَ رَبِّى فِى كِتَـٰبٍ ۖ لَّا يَضِلُّ رَبِّى وَلَا يَنسَى (٥۲)
He said, ‘Their knowledge is with my Lord, in a Book. My Lord neither makes any error nor forgets.’ (52)

Key Points

When Pharaoh asked about the state of previous nations and the deceased, Moses (عليه السلام), although He could have answered in detail, knew that the people were sensitive and protective of their ancestors. Therefore, in His reply, He spoke in general terms and briefly referred the knowledge of the matter to Allah. This ensured that He both acted upon the principle of "قولاً لیّناً" (a gentle word) and did not provide them with a means for excuse or objection.

Messages

1- The Prophets attributed the knowledge of whatever they did not know, or did not deem beneficial to disclose, to Allah. «علمها عند ربّى»

2- An educator must wisely answer some questions generally or with silence, so that the discussion does not become derailed or serve as a pretext for the enemy. «علمها عند ربّى»

3- In the Divine structure, everything is accounted for and calculated. «فى كتاب»

4- The Divine Book is extremely magnificent and great. The word «كتابٍ» is used in the indefinite form (nakirah).

5- Error has no way into Allah’s knowledge. «لایضّل ربّى و لاینسى» (Forgetfulness and error apply to those who do not know the reality, but concerning the God who knows the manifest and hidden aspects of everything exactly as they are, the possibility of error never exists.)

6- Human knowledge suffers from two defects: either one misunderstands from the outset and loses the truth, or one forgets after understanding it. However, Allah’s knowledge is free from these defects. «لایضّل ربّى و لا ینسى»

7- Only He has the right to Lordship (Rububiyyah) in whom deviation and forgetfulness have no place. «لایضّل ربّى و لاینسى»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ مَهْدًا وَسَلَكَ لَكُمْ فِيهَا سُبُلًا وَأَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِۦٓ أَزْوَٰجًا مِّن نَّبَاتٍ شَتَّىٰ (٥۳)
He, who made the earth for you a cradle, and in it threaded for you ways, and sent down water from the sky, and with it We brought forth various kinds of vegetation. (53)

Key Points

The phrase «سلك لكم» can be interpreted in two ways: A: The paths and roads that Allah has placed on Earth for human passage. B: The means of earning a livelihood. (78) When introducing Allah to Pharaoh, Prophet Moses عليه السلام said: Our Lord is He Who created everything and guided it. And in these verses, He points to examples of Divine guidance in nature: the Earth, which is the cradle of comfort for humanity; the earthly paths that are the means of communication and connection between regions; and the rain and various plants that carry the law of pairing (or duality/sexuality). We better recognize the blessings of Allah the Almighty when we assume the opposite. For example, if this Earth were not tranquil, and its movement was too slow or too fast, if rain did not fall or the Earth did not absorb it, if there were no vegetation or springs in it, or... would it still be considered a "cradle" (مَهد) ?!

78) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- Just as a child grows in the cradle (mahd) for the life outside of it, so too must man grow in the cradle of the Earth for the life outside the Earth, which is the Hereafter. (79) «مهداً»

2- The Earth is a resting place for man; it provides all his needs, carries his dead and his living, and is subjugated to him by Divine command. «مهداً»

3- The creation and conditions of the Earth are purposeful and for the benefit of man. «جعل لكم... سلك لكم»

4- The system of creation is based on the principle of causality (cause and effect). «فاخرجنا به»

5- Attention to the law of pairing (or duality/sexuality) in plants is among the scientific miracles of the Quran. (80) «ازواجا من نبات»

79) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

80) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Ayahs

كُلُوا۟ وَٱرْعَوْا۟ أَنْعَـٰمَكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلنُّهَىٰ (٥٤)
‘Eat and pasture your cattle.’ There are indeed signs in that for those who have sense. (54)
۞ مِنْهَا خَلَقْنَـٰكُمْ وَفِيهَا نُعِيدُكُمْ وَمِنْهَا نُخْرِجُكُمْ تَارَةً أُخْرَىٰ (٥٥)
From it did We create you, into it shall We return you, and from it shall We bring you forth another time. (55)

Key Points

Nuhā (نُهى) is the plural of nuhyah (نُهیه), derived from the root nahā (نَهى), meaning to restrain or forbid. Ulī al-Nuhā (اولى النُهى) means the possessors of intellect and wisdom. Intellect is called nuhyah because it restrains man from following his desires. (81) The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was asked, "Who are the Ulī al-Nuhā?" He replied: "They are those who possess good morals... and are dutiful to mothers and fathers, and who care for the poor, the neighbors, and the orphans, and who feed the hungry and spread peace throughout the world, and who pray while people are asleep and heedless." (82) The Commander of the Faithful, Ali عليه السلام, considered the secret of the prostrations (sujūd) in prayer to be allusions to this verse, and He said: The meaning of the first prostration is: O God! I was initially from this dust. When you raise your head from the prostration, it indicates that You created me from dust. The second prostration conveys the concept that You will return me to the dust, and rising from it means that You will resurrect me from the dust once again on the Day of Judgment. (83)

81) Tafsir al-Mizan. 82) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 83) Bihar, Vol. 85, p. 132.

Messages

  1. The creation of vegetation is also purposeful. «نبات شتّى، كلوا و ارعوا»
  2. The diversity of vegetation is for the diversity of needs. «نبات شتّى، كلوا و ارعوا»
  3. Livestock were created for mankind. «انعامكم»
  4. Utilizing material things should not make you heedless of the remembrance of Allah. «كلوا... اِنّ فى ذلك لآیات»
  5. The source and substance of human creation is from the earth. «منها خلقناكم»
  6. Reflection upon nature is one of the paths to knowing Allah. «لآیات لاولى النّهى»
  7. More important than possessing intellect is utilizing and following it. «لآیات لاولى النّهى»
  8. Man must be buried in the dust. «فیها نعیدكم»
  9. The Resurrection (Ma'ād) is physical. «نخرجكم تارة اُخرى»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ أَرَيْنَـٰهُ ءَايَـٰتِنَا كُلَّهَا فَكَذَّبَ وَأَبَىٰ (٥٦)
Certainly We showed him all Our signs. But he denied [them] and refused [to believe them]. (56)
قَالَ أَجِئْتَنَا لِتُخْرِجَنَا مِنْ أَرْضِنَا بِسِحْرِكَ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ (٥۷)
He said, ‘Moses, have you come to us to expel us from our land with your magic? (57)

Messages

1- Allah the Almighty completes the argument (establishes proof) even for Pharaohs. «و لقد اَریناه آیاتنا كلّها»

2- For some deniers, one miracle is sufficient, but for a tyrant like Pharaoh, multiple miracles must be presented. «اریناه آیاتنا كلّها»

3- Whenever the heart and soul of a person become sick, no evidence can satisfy him. «و لقد اَریناه آیاتنا كلّها فكذّب و اَبى»

4- An obstinate person is equivalent to Iblis (Satan). «فكذّب و ابى» Just as Allah says concerning Satan in Ayah 34 of Surah Al-Baqarah: «ابى و استكبر»

5- Tyrants, in times of helplessness, exploit emotions and use the weapon of patriotism to incite the feelings of the people. «لتخرجنا من أرضنا»

6- Slander and creating suspicion against the men of God is an arrogant method. «لتخرجنا من أرضنا بسحرك»

Ayahs

فَلَنَأْتِيَنَّكَ بِسِحْرٍ مِّثْلِهِۦ فَٱجْعَلْ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكَ مَوْعِدًا لَّا نُخْلِفُهُۥ نَحْنُ وَلَآ أَنتَ مَكَانًا سُوًى (٥۸)
Yet we [too] will bring you a magic like it! So fix a tryst between us and you, which neither we shall fail nor you, at a middle place.’ (58)
قَالَ مَوْعِدُكُمْ يَوْمُ ٱلزِّينَةِ وَأَن يُحْشَرَ ٱلنَّاسُ ضُحًى (٥۹)
He said, ‘Your tryst shall be the Day of Adornment, and let the people be assembled in early forenoon.’ (59)
فَتَوَلَّىٰ فِرْعَوْنُ فَجَمَعَ كَيْدَهُۥ ثُمَّ أَتَىٰ (٦٠)
Then Pharaoh withdrew [to consult privately], summoned up his guile, and then arrived [at the scene of the contest]. (60)

Key Points

One of the best actions of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) was transforming the national holiday (Eid) into a purposeful and sublime day and gathering, such that on that day, all the sorcerers changed their minds and beliefs upon seeing his miracle.

Messages

1- Belittling, slandering, and normalizing important events is the method of the tyrants (Taghuts). «فلنأتینّك بسحر مثله»

2- Sometimes, even when there is a feeling of emptiness within, a posture of power and decisiveness is adopted. «فلنأتینّك...»

3- Even if we make a promise to an enemy, we must fulfill it. «موعداً لا نخلفه»

4- Justice and fairness are good, even concerning the enemy. «مكاناً سوى» (The distance to that place should be equal for both groups, and there should be no advantage in it.)

5- Make the best use of periods of leisure. «موعدكم یوم الزّینة»

6- If guidance (Irshad) is accompanied by beauty, adornment, and the people's happiness, it will have a greater effect. «موعدكم یوم الزّینة»

7- The issue of Eid (holiday) and adornment has a very long history. «یوم الزّینة»

8- Logical and open discussions must take place in the presence of the people. «أن یحشر الناس ضحىً»

9- In propagation (Tabligh), the elements of time and place should not be neglected. «یوم الزّینة... ضُحىً... مكاناً سوى» (The people's leisure, «یوم الزّینة», the people's accessibility, «مكاناً سوى», and the brightness and pleasantness of the weather, «ضُحى», were all combined in Moses's propagation.)

10- The enemies of the Truth (Haqq), at the earliest possible opportunity, are plotting and scheming to overthrow the Truth. «فتولّى... فجمع كیده»

Ayahs

قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰ وَيْلَكُمْ لَا تَفْتَرُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا فَيُسْحِتَكُم بِعَذَابٍ ۖ وَقَدْ خَابَ مَنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ (٦۱)
Moses said to them, ‘Woe to you! Do not fabricate lies against Allah, or He will obliterate you with a punishment. Whoever fabricates lies certainly fails.’ (61)
فَتَنَـٰزَعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَهُم بَيْنَهُمْ وَأَسَرُّوا۟ ٱلنَّجْوَىٰ (٦۲)
So they disputed their matter among themselves, and kept their confidential talks secret. (62)

Messages

1- Considering the miracle as sorcery is slander against Allah. «لا تفتروا على اللّه»

2- Before performing the miracle, warning and informing the people is necessary. Moses عليه السلام said among the crowd: «لا تفتروا على اللّه»

3- The one who slanders Allah remains unsuccessful in this world and receives punishment and torment in the Hereafter. «فیسحتكم بعذاب و قد خاب من افترى»

4- We will never achieve results through lies and slander. «و قد خاب من افترى»

5- Whoever stands against the truth will be destroyed.(84) «و قد خاب من افترى»

6- You should enjoin good and forbid evil; if it does not affect certain individuals, it will at least create doubt and uncertainty in others. «لا تفتروا... فتنازعوا امرهم...»

7- Enemies conceal their disagreements. «فتنازعوا امرهم واسرّوا النجوى»۸۴) نهج البلاغه، ص ۵۸.

Ayahs

قَالُوٓا۟ إِنْ هَـٰذَٰنِ لَسَـٰحِرَٰنِ يُرِيدَانِ أَن يُخْرِجَاكُم مِّنْ أَرْضِكُم بِسِحْرِهِمَا وَيَذْهَبَا بِطَرِيقَتِكُمُ ٱلْمُثْلَىٰ (٦۳)
They said, ‘These two are indeed magicians who intend to expel you from your land with their magic, and to abolish your excellent tradition! (63)
فَأَجْمِعُوا۟ كَيْدَكُمْ ثُمَّ ٱئْتُوا۟ صَفًّا ۚ وَقَدْ أَفْلَحَ ٱلْيَوْمَ مَنِ ٱسْتَعْلَىٰ (٦٤)
So summon up your ingenuity, then come in ranks. Today he who has the upper hand will be felicitous!’ (64)

Key Points

Officials and rulers play a significant role in the beliefs of the people. Just as Pharaoh told Moses in Ayah 57: "You want to expel us from our land with your magic," in this Ayah, his followers say the same thing. In the logic of the men of God, victory belongs to the pious, "قدافلح من تزكّى" (85), while the slogan of the superpowers is seeking superiority and dominance, "قد افلح الیوم من استعلى".

(85) Al-A'la, 14.

Messages

1- Tyrants (Taghuts), in order to incite the people against the men of God, portray them as aggressive elements opposed to the national tradition/religion. "یخرجاكم من ارضكم... یذهبا بطریقتكم المثلى"

2- Ownership is a natural desire, and people fight against those who oppose it. "یخرجاكم من ارضكم" (Love of homeland is an innate matter and the desire of all people.)

3- Unity is the secret to victory, and discord is the secret to downfall. "اجمعوا كیدكم" (Gather all your schemes.) Pharaoh's followers called upon all their companions to cooperate and utilize every trick.

4- Order (discipline) is one of the factors that creates awe in the face of enemies. "صفّا"

5- To suppress the truth, enemies both unify their goal and coordinate the structure of their movement. "اجمعوا... صفّاً"

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِمَّآ أَن تُلْقِىَ وَإِمَّآ أَن نَّكُونَ أَوَّلَ مَنْ أَلْقَىٰ (٦٥)
They said, ‘O Moses! Will you throw first, or shall we?’ (65)
قَالَ بَلْ أَلْقُوا۟ ۖ فَإِذَا حِبَالُهُمْ وَعِصِيُّهُمْ يُخَيَّلُ إِلَيْهِ مِن سِحْرِهِمْ أَنَّهَا تَسْعَىٰ (٦٦)
He said, ‘No, you throw first.’ Thereupon, behold, their ropes and staffs appeared to him by their magic to wriggle swiftly. (66)
فَأَوْجَسَ فِى نَفْسِهِۦ خِيفَةً مُّوسَىٰ (٦۷)
Then Moses felt a fear within his heart. (67)

Key Points

«حِبال» (hibāl) is the plural of «حَبل» (habl), meaning rope, and «عصّى» ('iṣan) is the plural of «عَصا» ('aṣā), meaning staff. «اوجس» (awjasa) is derived from «وجس» (wajasa), meaning a hidden sound, and «ایجاس» (ījās) refers to something hidden within the inner self. Imam Ali عليه السلام, in Sermon 6 of Nahj al-Balagha, interpreting «فاوجس فى نفسه» (fā-awjasa fī nafsihī), states: The fear of Moses عليه السلام was concerning the effect of the magic upon the ignorant people.

Messages

1- Granting freedom (of choice) is not always a sign of truthfulness. «امّا ان تلقى وامّا...» (Either you cast, or...) (Pharaoh's supporters and the sorcerers gave Moses the freedom to choose one of the two paths.)

2- We should allow opponents freedom of action, and then refute them with a firm and logical response. «القوا» (Cast down!)

3- Allowing the presentation of doubts (or ambiguities) for the purpose of responding and resolving the issues is permissible. «القوا» (Cast down!)

4- Magic does not change the reality of things; rather, it alters appearances and affects human perception. «یخیلّ الیه» (it appears to him)

5- Leaders should not express their anxiety in the face of the enemy. «فاوجس فى نفسه» (So he conceived a fear in his soul)

6- The Prophet, like the general populace, experiences various states and emotions. «فاوجس فى نفسه» (So he conceived a fear in his soul)

Ayahs

قُلْنَا لَا تَخَفْ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ (٦۸)
We said, ‘Do not be afraid. Indeed, you will have the upper hand. (68)
وَأَلْقِ مَا فِى يَمِينِكَ تَلْقَفْ مَا صَنَعُوٓا۟ ۖ إِنَّمَا صَنَعُوا۟ كَيْدُ سَـٰحِرٍ ۖ وَلَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلسَّاحِرُ حَيْثُ أَتَىٰ (٦۹)
Throw down what is in your right hand, and it will swallow what they have conjured. What they have conjured is only a magician’s trick, and the magician does not fare well wherever he may show up.’ (69)
فَأُلْقِىَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سُجَّدًا قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ هَـٰرُونَ وَمُوسَىٰ (۷٠)
Thereat the magicians fell down prostrating. They said, ‘We have believed in the Lord of Aaron and Moses!’ (70)

Key Points

As soon as the sorcerers realized that the work of Moses (عليه السلام) was not magic or sorcery, they involuntarily fell into prostration, and while they were counted among the disbelievers in the morning, by sunset they joined the ranks of the martyrs in the way of Allah. By stating, «آمنّا بربّ هارون و موسى» (We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses), they declared that their previous work had been wrong. And since Pharaoh considered himself the "Lord" (ربّ) of the people, they used the phrase «رّب هارون و موسى» (Lord of Aaron and Moses). This is because if they had only said, "Lord of Moses," Pharaoh would have claimed, "I raised Moses." Therefore, the sorcerers, after the word "Lord" (ربّ), first mentioned the name of Aaron (عليه السلام) and then the name of Moses (عليه السلام).(86) Allah promises superiority to His Prophet unconditionally, «انّك انت الاعلى» (Indeed, you are the superior), but He promises superiority to the believers conditional upon their faithfulness to faith, «و انتم الاعلون ان كنتم مؤمنین» (And you will be superior if you are believers)(87). This is because the Prophets are faithful, but believers might abandon their faithfulness. Therefore, Allah states that they are superior on the condition of faithfulness.

86) Tafsir Maraghi. 87) Al ‘Imran, 139.

Messages

1- Wherever anxiety is greater, it requires more affirmation and emphasis. «لا تخف انّك انت الاعلى»(88)

2- Suggestion (or positive affirmation) is a factor in strengthening morale. «انّك انت الاعلى»

3- The response must be appropriate to the words of the disputant, and we should speak in his own language. Pharaoh said: «قدافلح الیوم مَن استعلى» (Successful indeed is he who overcomes today), and Allah said: «انّك انت الاعلى» (Indeed, you are the superior).

4- Truth destroys falsehood. «تلقف ما صنعوا» (It will swallow what they have produced), «انّ الباطل كان زهوقا»(89) (Indeed, falsehood is ever bound to vanish).

5- Not only the sorcerers of the time of Prophet Moses, but all sorcerers throughout history are wretched. «لا یفلح السّاحر»(90)

6- For sound and non-obstinate individuals, the realization of truth is simultaneous with submission. «اُلقى» (It is as if they were internally compelled to prostrate).

7- Man is a being who can decide and change his belief and ideology in an instant. «اُلقى السحرة»

8- The path of repentance is open to everyone; repentance from polytheism is believing in Allah. «اُلقى السحرة» We should not consider every misguided person to be bad; perhaps he will repent.

9- Humans are free in choosing their belief and are not compelled by their environment. «القى السحرة»

10- Prostration is among the effects of certainty; faith cannot exist without prostration. «القى السحرة سجّدا»

11- The sorcerers expressed their faith in Monotheism (Tawhid) and Prophethood in a single sentence. «آمنّا برّب هارون و موسى» (The goldsmith knows the value of gold, and the jeweler knows the value of the jewel.)

88) The inclusion of the particle of emphasis «انّ», the repetition of the connected and disconnected pronouns of address «ك and انت», along with the word for superiority (علّو) in the form of «اعلى» and the nominal sentence structure, all serve as affirmation against those anxieties.

89) Al-Isra, 81.

90) The alif and lam (definite article) in the word «الساحر» denote generality.

Ayah

قَالَ ءَامَنتُمْ لَهُۥ قَبْلَ أَنْ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ ٱلَّذِى عَلَّمَكُمُ ٱلسِّحْرَ ۖ فَلَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُم مِّنْ خِلَـٰفٍ وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمْ فِى جُذُوعِ ٱلنَّخْلِ وَلَتَعْلَمُنَّ أَيُّنَآ أَشَدُّ عَذَابًا وَأَبْقَىٰ (۷۱)
He said, ‘Did you believe in him before I should permit you? He is indeed your chief who has taught you magic! Surely, I will cut off your hands and feet from opposite sides, and I will crucify you on the trunks of palm trees, and you will know which of us can inflict a severer and more lasting punishment.’ (71)

Key Points

In Ayah 61, Moses threatened the Pharaohites with punishment, [saying] «لاتفتروا على اللّه فیسحتكم بعذاب». In this Ayah, Pharaoh says: soon you will know whose punishment is severer.

Messages

1- The enemy becomes a cause for good if Allah wills it. «قال آمنتم» Pharaoh had gathered all the experts to destroy the truth, but all of them were guided by the hand of Moses عليه السلام.

2- In a tyrannical system, suppression reigns, and people are deprived even of freedom of belief. «آمنتم له قبل ان اذن لكم»

3- Threat and accusation are the methods of tyrants. «انّه لكبیركم»

4- Prophets work with the human soul, but tyrants take revenge by striking the body. «لاقطّعن...» They are unaware that the beliefs and convictions of the people cannot be changed through torture and murder.

5- Arrogant oppressors imagine themselves to be eternal. «ایّنا اشدّ عذاباً وابقى»

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ لَن نُّؤْثِرَكَ عَلَىٰ مَا جَآءَنَا مِنَ ٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَٱلَّذِى فَطَرَنَا ۖ فَٱقْضِ مَآ أَنتَ قَاضٍ ۖ إِنَّمَا تَقْضِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَآ (۷۲)
They said, ‘We will never prefer you to the clear proofs which have come to us, and to Him who originated us. Decide whatever you want to decide. You can only decide about the life of this world. (72)
إِنَّآ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّنَا لِيَغْفِرَ لَنَا خَطَـٰيَـٰنَا وَمَآ أَكْرَهْتَنَا عَلَيْهِ مِنَ ٱلسِّحْرِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقَىٰٓ (۷۳)
We have indeed believed in our Lord that He may forgive us our offences and the magic you compelled us to perform. Allah is better and more lasting.’ (73)

Key Points

Khataayaa (خطایا) is the plural of Khaṭī’ah (خطیئه), and it refers to intentional sins. (91) The phrase «ما اكرهتنا علیه من السحر» means we seek forgiveness from Allah for the sins (mistakes) that you forced us into. And the intent of being forced into sorcery (ikrāh ‘alā al-siḥr) is the summoning of the sorcerers and their act of sorcery. (92) Whoever sacrifices his life in the path of the Divine goal achieves greater growth every moment. The sorcerers in these verses used three types of expressions regarding Allah: «الّذى فطرنا» (Who created us), «آمنّا بربّنا» (We believed in our Lord), and «واللّه خیر و ابقى» (And Allah is better and more lasting). In a tradition from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, we read: Whoever considers himself superior to others is arrogant (mustakbir). They asked: Are pure individuals who consider themselves better than sinners arrogant? The Imam explained the story of the sorcerers embracing faith, stating that a person sometimes changes his mind in a few moments; therefore, do not consider yourselves better, as you do not know the final outcome and destiny. (93)

91) Lisān al-‘Arab. 92) If coercion (ikrāh) is such that a person's free will is removed, there is no need for apology or seeking forgiveness (istighfār). Therefore, the sorcerers' seeking forgiveness for the coercion indicates that Pharaoh's coercion was not to the extent of removing their free will. 93) Kāfī, Vol. 8, p. 328.

Messages

1- Humans are free and possess choice in belief and faith, and their convictions cannot be changed through threats or temptation. «لاقطعنّ، لاصلبنّ... لن نؤثرك»

2- Make your enemies despair of you. «لن نؤثرك»

3- Faith grants a person power and courage. After embracing faith, they said: «لن نؤثرك»

4- Faith is valuable when it is based on logic and insight. «لن نؤثرك على ماجاءنا من البیّنات»

5- Superpowers are insignificant and worthless in the eyes of true believers. «لن نؤثرك»

6- The sign of true faith is readiness to sacrifice one's entire existence in defense of Divine values. «ما جاءنا من البینات... فاقض ما انت قاض»

7- Whoever lacks faith is attracted by the tyrant's gift, «ءَانّ لنا لاجراً» (Will there be a reward for us?), but the believer considers everything other than Allah small and worthless. «انماتقضى هذه الحیوة الدنیا»

8- The first step toward Divine pardon and forgiveness is faith in Him. «امنّا بربنا لیغفر لنا»

9- Sorcery is a sin and must be repented for. «لیغفرلنا خطایانا... من السحر»

10- Tyrants (Tāghūts) exploit people's expertise. «مااكرهتنا علیه من السحر»

11- Retaliate against enemies in kind. Pharaoh said: «لتعلمنّ ایّنا اشد عذاباً و ابقى» (You will surely know which of us is more severe in punishment and more lasting), and the sorcerers who had believed said: «واللّه خیر و ابقى» (And Allah is better and more lasting).

Ayahs

إِنَّهُۥ مَن يَأْتِ رَبَّهُۥ مُجْرِمًا فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ جَهَنَّمَ لَا يَمُوتُ فِيهَا وَلَا يَحْيَىٰ (۷٤)
Whoever comes to his Lord laden with guilt, for him shall be hell, where he will neither live nor die. (74)
وَمَن يَأْتِهِۦ مُؤْمِنًا قَدْ عَمِلَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمُ ٱلدَّرَجَـٰتُ ٱلْعُلَىٰ (۷٥)
But whoever comes to Him with faith and he has done righteous deeds, for such shall be the highest ranks (75)
جَنَّـٰتُ عَدْنٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ (۷٦)
—the Gardens of Eden, with streams running in them, to abide in them [forever], and that is the reward of him who keeps pure. (76)

Key Points

After believing, the sorcerers told Pharaoh: Your application of torture and killing is only for these few days of this world, while Allah's wrath or grace is permanent. Both the inhabitants of Hell are eternal in Hell, «لایموت فیها و لایحیى», and the inhabitants of Paradise are eternal in Paradise. «جنات عدن... خالدین فیها» The abode of the believer is Paradise, but attaining rank relates to the extent of his purification (tazkiyah), for which he must strive, purifying and cleansing himself from impurities in all dimensions: purification of the soul from deviant beliefs, purification of character from moral vices, purification of the body from impurities (khaba'ith), and purification of deeds and conduct from ugly and inappropriate actions.

Messages

1- Whoever does not repent will be resurrected as a criminal. «من یأت ربه مجرماً» (The sorcerers said: We believed so that Allah would accept our repentance and forgive us. Then they said: Whoever comes to Allah as a criminal—meaning without faith and repentance for past deeds—Hell is his abode.)

2- Worse than sin is dying as a sinner. «یأت ربّه مجرماً»

3- Man must preserve his faith until the end of his life. «یأته مؤمناً»

4- Man is free in choosing his belief. «من یأت ربّه مجرماً... من یأته مؤمناً»
