5- Faith is inseparable from righteous deeds. «مؤمناً قد عمل الصالحات»

6- The true believer is a doer of every righteous deed. «عمل الصالحات»

7- Paradise possesses a hierarchy of ranks. «درجات العلى»

8- Purification (Tazkiyah) is the key to Paradise. «و ذلك جزاء من تزكّى»

9- The believer is dedicated to self-building and purification. «مؤمناً... لهم الدرجات... جزاء من تزكّى»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنْ أَسْرِ بِعِبَادِى فَٱضْرِبْ لَهُمْ طَرِيقًا فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ يَبَسًا لَّا تَخَـٰفُ دَرَكًا وَلَا تَخْشَىٰ (۷۷)
We revealed to Moses, [saying], ‘Set out with My servants at night, and strike out for them a dry path through the sea. Do not be afraid of being overtaken, and have no fear [of getting drowned]. (77)
فَأَتْبَعَهُمْ فِرْعَوْنُ بِجُنُودِهِۦ فَغَشِيَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْيَمِّ مَا غَشِيَهُمْ (۷۸)
Then Pharaoh pursued them with his troops, whereat they were engulfed by what engulfed them of the sea. (78)
وَأَضَلَّ فِرْعَوْنُ قَوْمَهُۥ وَمَا هَدَىٰ (۷۹)
Pharaoh led his people astray and did not guide them. (79)

Key Points

When the sorcerers and the people of Egypt believed in Moses (عليه السلام) and were not afraid of Pharaoh's threats, the path of Divine aid was opened. Therefore, Moses (عليه السلام) was addressed to move the people out of Egypt at night, so that We would dry the sea for you and keep the evil of Pharaoh away from you. (94) Isrā’ (اسراء) means movement during the night. Yabas (یَبَس) refers to a place that previously had water but is now dry. And Darak (دَرَك) refers to the losses that afflict a person, and the intent of ‘Ibādī (عبادى) is the Children of Israel.

94) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Qur'an.

Messages

1- The movement of the Prophets and their method of struggle originate from Divine revelation. «أوحينا»

2- Honor accompanied by displacement is better than humiliation in one's homeland. «أسرِ بعبادى»

3- If we cannot have an effect in an environment, we should at least leave that environment and migrate from it. «أسرِ بعبادى»

4- Among the goals of the Prophets' mission is saving people from the evil of the tyrants (Taghuts). «أسر بعبادى»

5- The first step against the oppressor is emptying his surroundings. «أسر بعبادى»

6- Allah is the Helper of the deprived. «عبادى»

7- The guidance and administration of the people are the responsibility of the Divine leaders. «أسرِ بعبادى فاضرب»

8- The miracle of Moses (عليه السلام) shook the society and compelled movement to such an extent that Pharaoh himself was forced to pursue them. «فاتبعهم فرعون»

9- The sea, like all of nature, is commissioned by Allah's wrath or His mercy. «فغشيَهم»

10- Leaders and rulers of society play a key role in the guidance or misguidance of the people. «أضلّ فرعون قومه و ما هَدى»

11- Do not leave misleading slogans unanswered. Pharaoh used to say: «مَا أَهْدِيكُمْ إِلَّا سَبِيلَ الرَّشَادِ» (95) But Allah says: «وَأَضَلَّ فِرْعَوْنُ قَوْمَهُ وَمَا هَدَى» Pharaoh not only failed to guide the people but also led them astray. 95) Ghafir, 29.

Ayah

يَـٰبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ قَدْ أَنجَيْنَـٰكُم مِّنْ عَدُوِّكُمْ وَوَٰعَدْنَـٰكُمْ جَانِبَ ٱلطُّورِ ٱلْأَيْمَنَ وَنَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْمَنَّ وَٱلسَّلْوَىٰ (۸٠)
O Children of Israel! We delivered you from your enemy, and We appointed with you a tryst on the right side of the Mount and We sent down to you manna and quails: (80)

Key Points

The issue of the salvation of the Children of Israel from the oppression of Pharaoh and the descent of the foods mann and salwa while they were wandering in the desert has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran. Mann (مَنّ) is apparently honey and turanjabin, and salwa (سَلوى) is a type of delicious, lawful bird. The phrase, "واعدناكم من جانب الطور" (We appointed a time for you on the side of the Mount [Tur]), refers to the event of Prophet Moses, accompanied by a group of the Children of Israel, going to the appointed place on Mount Tur, where Allah revealed the Tablets of the Torah upon Moses.

Messages

1- Reminding people of divine blessings is one of the duties of the prophets and provides the basis for human growth and gratitude. "يا بني إسرائيل قد أنجيناكم..." (O Children of Israel, We have saved you...)

2- Freedom and security are among the greatest divine blessings and pave the way for the utilization of other blessings. "أنجيناكم..." (We saved you...)

3- After the fall of tyranny and the establishment of government, the most important need is law. "أنجيناكم... واعدناكم" (We saved you... and We appointed a time for you) (Allah's promise was the bestowal of the Torah and divine law).

4- All favors originate from Allah. "أنجينا - واعدنا - نزلّنا" (We saved - We appointed - We sent down).

5- Spiritual blessings precede material blessings. First, He said, "واعدناكم" (We appointed a time for you), which relates to the descent of the Torah, and then He said, "المنّ والسّلوى" (the mann and the salwa), which is physical sustenance.

Ayah

كُلُوا۟ مِن طَيِّبَـٰتِ مَا رَزَقْنَـٰكُمْ وَلَا تَطْغَوْا۟ فِيهِ فَيَحِلَّ عَلَيْكُمْ غَضَبِى ۖ وَمَن يَحْلِلْ عَلَيْهِ غَضَبِى فَقَدْ هَوَىٰ (۸۱)
‘Eat of the good things We have provided you, but do not overstep the bounds therein, lest My wrath should descend on you, and he on whom My wrath descends certainly perishes. (81)

Messages

1- The default principle regarding things that are tayyib (good) and pure is the permissibility of using them. «كلوا من طیّبات» (Eat of the good things). Therefore, prohibiting the tayyibāt is forbidden.

2- Divine religions pay attention to nutritional health. «كلوامن طیّبات...» (Eat of the good things...)

3- Sound human nature is one of the criteria for recognizing lawful things (halāl). «طیبّات» (The tayyib is that which conforms to one's nature and is pleasing.)

4- Failing to observe the rulings regarding food is one of the grounds for transgression (tughyān). «كلوا... لاتطغوا» (Eat... and do not transgress.)

5- Nations that have been freed from oppression are at risk of transgression. «انجیناكم...كلوا... و لاتطغوا» (We saved you... eat... and do not transgress.)

6- We should not consume the lawful things (halāl) granted by Allah in an unlawful manner. «لا تطغوا فیه» (Do not transgress therein.)

7- Consuming more than the necessary amount is disobedience to the command of Allah. «لا تطغوا فیه» (Do not transgress therein.)

8- Divine wrath against transgressors is a serious threat. «فیحلّ» (Then My wrath shall descend.)

9- Transgression in consumption is among the major sins, as it entails Divine punishment. «لاتطغوا - فیحلّ علیكم غضبى» (Do not transgress - then My wrath shall descend upon you.)

10- The true downfall is being afflicted by Divine wrath, not political, economic, or similar bankruptcies. «من یحلل علیه غضبى فقد هوى» (He upon whom My wrath descends has certainly fallen.)

Ayah

وَإِنِّى لَغَفَّارٌ لِّمَن تَابَ وَءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا ثُمَّ ٱهْتَدَىٰ (۸۲)
Indeed I forgive those who repent, become faithful, act righteously, and follow guidance.’ (82)

Key Points

Following the previous Ayah, which concluded with a threat, this Ayah brings divine glad tidings, and this method is seen throughout the Qur'an. Repentance (Tawbah) for every sin and transgression must be commensurate with that sin and transgression. For example, the repentance of one who has not prayed is performing the missed prayers (Qada'), the repentance for harming people is apologizing, the repentance for concealing truths is revealing them, the repentance for polytheism (Shirk) is faith in Allah, and the repentance for consuming people's wealth unjustly is returning the properties to their owners. Although this Ayah speaks of the forgiveness of those who repent, in another Ayah we read: Those who disbelieve after their belief and then increase in disbelief, their repentance will never be accepted. «اِنّ الّذین كفروا بعد ایمانهم ثمّ ازدادوا كفراً لن تقبل توبتهم»(96) The Divine Prophets also sought forgiveness from Allah; Adam عليه السلام: «اِن لم تغفرلنا»(97), Noah عليه السلام: «الاّ تغفرلى وترحمنى»(98), Abraham عليه السلام: «اطمع أن یغفرلى»(99), Moses عليه السلام: «ربّ اغفرلى و لاخى»(100), Jesus عليه السلام: «وأن تغفرلهم»(101) and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله: «واستغفره»(102) In narrations, the meaning intended by «اِهتدى» (is guided) is understood to be guidance towards the Wilayah (guardianship) of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام.(103)

96) Aal-i-Imran, 90. 97) Al-A'raf, 23. 98) Hud, 47. 99) Ash-Shu'ara, 82. 100) Al-A'raf, 151. 101) Al-Ma'idah, 118. 102) An-Nasr, 4. 103) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 393.

Messages

1- The path of repentance is open to those who have incurred Divine wrath. «فقد هوى...اِنّى لغفّار»

2- A person's true return (repentance) brings about forgiveness and many effects from Allah. «انّى لغفّار لمن تاب»

3- Repentance is accepted under certain conditions. A: Return/Repentance («تاب»), B: Faith («آمن»), C: Good deed («عمل صالحاً»), D: Acceptance of guidance («اهتدى»).

4- Even faith and good deeds are not sufficient without accepting guidance from the Divine guides. «آمن و عمل صالحاً ثمّ اهتدى» (Indeed, if there is faith and good deeds, but a person does not follow the line of guidance set by the Divine guides, he will become prey to the Samiris and Bal'am Ba'uras.)

5- Being a believer and becoming a believer is important, but remaining a believer is more important. «ثم اهتدى»

Ayahs

۞ وَمَآ أَعْجَلَكَ عَن قَوْمِكَ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ (۸۳)
[God said,] ‘O Moses, what has prompted you to hasten ahead of your people?’ (83)
قَالَ هُمْ أُو۟لَآءِ عَلَىٰٓ أَثَرِى وَعَجِلْتُ إِلَيْكَ رَبِّ لِتَرْضَىٰ (۸٤)
He said, ‘They are close upon my heels, and I hurried on to You, my Lord, that You may be pleased.’ (84)
قَالَ فَإِنَّا قَدْ فَتَنَّا قَوْمَكَ مِنۢ بَعْدِكَ وَأَضَلَّهُمُ ٱلسَّامِرِىُّ (۸٥)
He said, ‘Indeed We tried your people in your absence, and the Samiri has led them astray.’ (85)

Key Points

| Imam Sadiq عليه السلام described the characteristics of the lover (of God) concerning the above verses, saying: The yearning person is not concerned with food, clothing, or shelter, nor does he have peace or rest until he reaches his Beloved. Just as Prophet Moses, out of love for receiving revelation, had no sleep or food, and said: O God! I have hastened to You sooner than my people so that You may be pleased. (104)

(104) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- The Prophet is also responsible for his actions. «ما اعجلك...»

2- The leader must be a pioneer so that the nation follows him. «اولاء على اثرى»

3- Moses عليه السلام was a lover of God and yearned to hear the Divine Word. «عجلت الیك»

4- Hastening in good deeds and for the sake of attaining Divine pleasure is permissible. «عجلت ... لترضى»

5- The highest goal of the prophets and their ultimate destination is to gain Divine satisfaction. «لترضى»

6- When the leader is present in society, enemies cannot achieve anything. Deviants exploit the absence or lack of a Divine leader. «من بعدك»

7- Nations are exposed to trials and tribulations (Fitnah) in the absence of their leaders. «فتنّا...من بعدك»

8- The role of art and the non-committed artist in destroying religious beliefs and misleading people is no less significant than the power of the tyrant (Taghut). «اضلّهم السامرى»

9- Apostasy is a serious danger and threat for those who have recently embraced faith. «اضلّهم السامرى»

10- Being companions of the Prophet is not enough; a good outcome (Husn al-'Aqibah) and religious steadfastness are necessary. «اضلّهم السامرّى» (Allah saved the Children of Israel and provided them with good things, but they ended up with a bad outcome.)

Ayah

فَرَجَعَ مُوسَىٰٓ إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ غَضْبَـٰنَ أَسِفًا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَلَمْ يَعِدْكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ وَعْدًا حَسَنًا ۚ أَفَطَالَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْعَهْدُ أَمْ أَرَدتُّمْ أَن يَحِلَّ عَلَيْكُمْ غَضَبٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ فَأَخْلَفْتُم مَّوْعِدِى (۸٦)
Thereupon Moses returned to his people, indignant and grieved. He said, ‘O my people! Did your Lord not give you a true promise? Did the period [of my absence] seem too long to you? Or did you desire that your Lord’s wrath should descend on you and so you failed your tryst with me?’ (86)

Key Points

This Ayah refers to two promises: one promise from Allah, which was the revelation of the Torah, «الم یعدكم ربّكم وعداً حسنا» (Did your Lord not promise you a good promise?); and the second, a promise made by Moses عليه السلام to the people that they should obey his brother Aaron during his absence. However, the people disregarded both by worshipping the calf. Of course, when Moses rebuked them, he asked the reason for their deviation, inquiring: Was your deviation deliberate, and did you knowingly seek Allah's wrath? Or did you turn to it out of negligence? Did you deviate because my thirty-day absence extended to forty days? And so on... (105) In this Ayah, just like Ayah 81, the descent of Divine wrath is mentioned. However, in that Ayah, the cause of the wrath was transgression, while here it is the people's breach of covenant. These are the factors in which the secret of the downfall of the Children of Israel is hidden.

105) Tafsir Maraghi.

Messages

1- As soon as you become aware of a deviation, react immediately. «فرجع» (The letter 'fa' (ف) in «فرجع» indicates immediacy.)

2- Religious zeal (Ghayrah) is a prominent characteristic of Allah's chosen servants (Awliya). «غضبان اسفا» (Religious leaders and Allah's Awliya grieve over the deviation of the people.)

3- Anger and wrath that are for the sake of Divine pleasure are not blameworthy. «غضبان اسفا» (The anger and sorrow of Allah's Awliya are due to the apostasy and spiritual deviation of the people.)

4- The wrongdoer must be rebuked when appropriate. «اَلم یعدكم»

5- The absence of the leader, or minor changes in plans, should not cause deviation and apostasy. «أفطال علیكم العهد»

6- Regression, apostasy, and breach of covenant are causes of Allah's wrath and indignation. «یحلّ علیكم غضبى»

7- Indignation and wrath are necessities of nurturing (Tarbiyah). «غضب من ربكم»

8- Violating the covenant and promise of the Prophet is a cause of Allah's indignation. «یحل علیكم غضب... فاخلفتم موعدى» (There are commitments and agreements between the Prophets and the people. These agreements include their conduct (Sirah), rulings (Ahkam), and their representatives among the people, to which the people must remain faithful. Moses' agreement was obedience to Aaron.)

9- Disobeying the successor (Wasi) and representative of the Prophet is disobedience to the Prophet himself. «فاخلفتم موعدى» (Failure to follow Aaron is disobedience to Moses.) (106)

10- When rebuking, leave open a window for repentance and return for the wrongdoer. «أفطال علیكم العهد ام اردتم» (This message is derived from Moses' multiple questions.)

106) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan.

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ مَآ أَخْلَفْنَا مَوْعِدَكَ بِمَلْكِنَا وَلَـٰكِنَّا حُمِّلْنَآ أَوْزَارًا مِّن زِينَةِ ٱلْقَوْمِ فَقَذَفْنَـٰهَا فَكَذَٰلِكَ أَلْقَى ٱلسَّامِرِىُّ (۸۷)
They said, ‘We did not fail our tryst with you of our own accord, but we were laden with the weight of those people’s ornaments, and we cast them [into the fire] and so did the Samiri.’ (87)

Messages

1- Sinners resort to excuses like compulsion, coercion, and external pressure to escape punishment. «ما اخلفنا موعدك بملكنا»

2- Unlawful wealth is spent on unlawful deeds. «من زینة القوم» (The ornaments that the Pharaohs had acquired through oppression and which had been inherited by the Children of Israel were ultimately spent on making an idol and a calf.)(107)

3- How often are the displays, ornaments, and wealth of this world a burden and affliction for man. «اوزاراً»

4- Deviants use the capital of the people themselves to mislead them and achieve their sinister goals. «من زینة القوم»

5- A weak-faithed and submissive society loses everything through one artistic trick. «فكذلك القى السامرى»

6- The indoctrination by deviants and their artistic display are not without effect in society. «القى السامرى»

7- The criminal tries to shift the blame for the sin onto others. «القى السامرى»

8- The greatest blows are struck by deviant insiders. «السامرى»

(107) Tafsir Namuneh.

Ayahs

فَأَخْرَجَ لَهُمْ عِجْلًا جَسَدًا لَّهُۥ خُوَارٌ فَقَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَآ إِلَـٰهُكُمْ وَإِلَـٰهُ مُوسَىٰ فَنَسِىَ (۸۸)
Then he produced for them a calf—a [lifeless] body with a low—and they said, This is your god and the god of Moses, so he forgot! (88)
أَفَلَا يَرَوْنَ أَلَّا يَرْجِعُ إِلَيْهِمْ قَوْلًا وَلَا يَمْلِكُ لَهُمْ ضَرًّا وَلَا نَفْعًا (۸۹)
Did they not see that it did not answer them, nor could it bring them any benefit or harm? (89)

Key Points

One of the Samiri's tricks was that he did not show the people how the calf was made, and the people suddenly encountered a sounding calf figure. «فاخرج» The Children of Israel did not consider that if the calf was worthy of worship, the Samiri himself, who was its maker, was worthy of worship a fortiori.

Messages

1- Art that is accompanied by sociology and psychology is more effective. «فاخرج لهم عجلاً» (The factor in the Samiri's rapid success in attracting the attention of the Children of Israel was the recognition of the grounds for idolatry and cow worship in that society and the absence of the leader.)

2- Art in the possession of misguided individuals is deadly and dangerous, like a blade in the hand of a drunken man, just like the Samiri's calf. «القى السامرى»

3- Sculpture, metal melting, and casting have a long history. «عجلاًجسداً»

4- The greatest danger threatening any nation (Ummah) and revolution is reactionism (regression), apostasy, and cultural and ideological deviation. «هذا الهكم»

5- The innate nature (Fitrah) of all human beings is based on monotheism (worship of God). The Prophets علیهم السلام show the correct path and the true object of worship, but the Samiris show the wrong path and the calves. «هذا الهكم»

6- If the elders and leaders of a people are ruined by slander, the path is paved for ruining other individuals. «و اله موسى»

7- The result of forgetting divine decrees and commandments and separating from religious leaders is deviation and a bad end (consequence). «فنسى» (It is said: The Samiri was one of the companions of Moses علیه السلام who forgot his commitments to him.)(108)

8- The path and school of the Prophets is based on reasoning and logic. «أفلا یرون... لایملك لهم نفعاً و لا ضرّاً» (Reason dictates that the object of human worship must have the power to bring benefit to man and ward off harm from him.)

(108) Tafsir Namuneh.

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ قَالَ لَهُمْ هَـٰرُونُ مِن قَبْلُ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِنَّمَا فُتِنتُم بِهِۦ ۖ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ فَٱتَّبِعُونِى وَأَطِيعُوٓا۟ أَمْرِى (۹٠)
Aaron had certainly told them earlier, ‘O my people! You are only being tested by it. Indeed your Lord is the All-beneficent. So follow me and obey my command!’ (90)
قَالُوا۟ لَن نَّبْرَحَ عَلَيْهِ عَـٰكِفِينَ حَتَّىٰ يَرْجِعَ إِلَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ (۹۱)
They had said, ‘We will keep on clinging to it until Moses returns to us.’ (91)

Messages

1- Some are so lost that neither reason governs them ("أَفَلَا يَرَوْنَ") nor is the Prophet a guide for them ("قَالَ لَهُمْ هَارُونُ").

2- The deviation and apostasy of the Children of Israel were conscious acts. ("وَلَقَدْ قَالَ لَهُمْ هَارُونُ ...")

3- The duty of the leader and his followers, when innovation (bid'ah) arises, is to protest and complete the argument (itmam al-hujjah). ("وَلَقَدْ قَالَ لَهُمْ هَارُونُ")

4- Prophets are compassionate towards the people. ("وَلَقَدْ قَالَ لَهُمْ هَارُونُ... يَا قَوْمِ")

5- The testing of humans is a definite Divine tradition, but the means (of testing) differ. ("إِنَّمَا فُتِنْتُمْ بِهِ")

6- Reminding people of Divine grace and mercy can prepare the ground for their guidance and repentance. ("رَبُّكُمُ الرَّحْمَنُ")

7- Obedience to the successor (wasi) and representative of the Prophet is obligatory. ("فَاتَّبِعُونِي وَأَطِيعُوا أَمْرِي")

8- Following Divine leaders is a source of immunity from trials (fitan). ("فُتِنْتُمْ... وَأَطِيعُوا أَمْرِي")

9- For obstinate individuals, the protest of the Prophets is also ineffective. ("قَالُوا لَنْ نَبْرَحَ")

10- Instead of using reason, the Children of Israel were personality-oriented. ("حَتَّى يَرْجِعَ إِلَيْنَا مُوسَى")

Ayahs

قَالَ يَـٰهَـٰرُونُ مَا مَنَعَكَ إِذْ رَأَيْتَهُمْ ضَلُّوٓا۟ (۹۲)
He said, ‘O Aaron! What kept you, when you saw them going astray, (92)
أَلَّا تَتَّبِعَنِ ۖ أَفَعَصَيْتَ أَمْرِى (۹۳)
from following me? Did you disobey my command?’ (93)
قَالَ يَبْنَؤُمَّ لَا تَأْخُذْ بِلِحْيَتِى وَلَا بِرَأْسِىٓ ۖ إِنِّى خَشِيتُ أَن تَقُولَ فَرَّقْتَ بَيْنَ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَلَمْ تَرْقُبْ قَوْلِى (۹٤)
He said, ‘O son of my mother! Do not grab my beard or my head! I feared lest you should say, ‘‘You have caused a rift among the Children of Israel and did not heed my word [of advice].’’’ (94)

Key Points

When Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) returned from Mount Tur and saw his people deviated, he questioned three groups: A: The people ("يا قوم ألم يعدكم ربّكم"), B: Aaron (عليه السلام) ("يا هارون ما منعك..."), C: The Samiri ("فما خطبك يا سامرى"). According to Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan, since Aaron (عليه السلام) is a Prophet and infallible (ma'sum) and had fulfilled His duty of forbidding evil (nahy 'an al-munkar), the reprimand by Moses (عليه السلام) can be considered, metaphorically, a "goldsmith's quarrel" (a staged argument), where he speaks to the door so the wall may hear. He speaks to Aaron so that the people may take account of their actions. In Tafsir Safi, quoting Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام), it is stated that Moses's (عليه السلام) rebuke was because Aaron (عليه السلام), upon seeing that situation, did not immediately inform Moses. In the method of propagation (tabligh), sometimes it is necessary to undertake a new action to shake public opinion and send tremors through the dead body of society. This is exemplified by Moses (عليه السلام) having that harsh confrontation with his infallible successor, or by Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام) slapping Himself hard on His face while delivering a sermon, in order to draw the people's attention.

Messages

1- Leaders must be accountable for the deviations of the people. "يا هارون ما منعك"

2- The silence and indifference of officials are subject to reprimand. "ما منعك"

3- A bad outcome (in the Hereafter) is a plague for the religious. "رأيتهم ضلّوا"

4- One Prophet can be subordinate to another Prophet. "أفعصيت أمري"

5- Unified management must prevail in society. "أفعصيت أمري"

6- To extinguish the anger of others, we should use emotional words. "يابن أمّ"

7- Religion and piety are more important than kinship. "لا تأخذ بلحيتي"

8- Religious zeal and fervor are prerequisites for prophethood. "لا تأخذ بلحيتي"

9- We must give the accused an opportunity to defend himself. "لا تأخذ بلحيتي"

10- It is permissible for a man to have long hair and locks. "و لا برأسي"

11- The danger of disunity within a nation frightened even Prophet Aaron. "إنّي خشيت"

12- Preserving unity is more important than saving a single group. "إنّي خشيت... فرّقت"

13- In decision-making, attention must be paid to the issue of the more important and the important (prioritization). "إنّي خشيت"

14- In revolutionary confrontations, attention must be paid to the consequences of the action, and one must not be one-sided. "إنّي خشيت..."

15- The danger of causing division is more serious than the danger of silence in the face of deviation. "فرّقت بين بني إسرائيل..."

Ayahs

قَالَ فَمَا خَطْبُكَ يَـٰسَـٰمِرِىُّ (۹٥)
He said, ‘What is your business, O Samiri?’ (95)
قَالَ بَصُرْتُ بِمَا لَمْ يَبْصُرُوا۟ بِهِۦ فَقَبَضْتُ قَبْضَةً مِّنْ أَثَرِ ٱلرَّسُولِ فَنَبَذْتُهَا وَكَذَٰلِكَ سَوَّلَتْ لِى نَفْسِى (۹٦)
He said, ‘I saw what they did not see. I took a handful [of dust] from the messenger’s trail and threw it. That is how my soul prompted me.’ (96)

Key Points

The phrase "بصرت به" (I perceived/saw) is usually used in the context of basīrah (insight/understanding), the plural of which is baṣā’ir, and not in the context of baṣar (eye/sight), the plural of which is abṣār. (109)

It is narrated in the book Iḥtijāj Ṭabarsī that when Hadrat Ali عليه السلام conquered Basra, people gathered around him to hear his words. The Imam’s eye fell upon Hasan al-Basri among the crowd, who was writing something down. Imam عليه السلام addressed him loudly and asked, "What are you doing?" He replied, "I am writing down your words to recount them to others." Imam عليه السلام said: "Be aware that every nation and community has a Samiri, and you, O Hasan, are the Samiri of this Ummah! You take the traditions (āthār) of the Messenger of Allah from me, and then, using your own desires and interpretation based on personal opinion (tafsīr bi al-ra’y), you create a new school of thought and invite people to it." (110)

According to the commentaries Al-Mizan, Furqan, and Namūnah, the Samiri's intention behind "قَبَضتُ قبضة من أثر الرّسول" (I took a handful from the trace of the Messenger) is that he acquired some of the traditions of Moses and believed in them, then abandoned them and fashioned the calf. Consequently, the phrase "بَصُرتُ بمالم یبصروا" (I perceived what they did not perceive) means that I realized a plan for carrying out this deed that others were unaware of, and this meaning is more suitable given the aforementioned Hadith.

(109) Mufradāt Rāghib. (110) Tafsīr Namūnah.

Messages

1- To eliminate deviation, one must first investigate the root cause of the deviation. "فما خطبك یا سامرى" (What was your case, O Samiri?)

2- Deviants and cultural corruptors must be confronted. "فما خَطبُك یا سامرى"

3- In a revolution, not all people change internally; rather, individuals like the Samiris wait for an opportunity. "فما خطبك یا سامرى"

4- Sometimes deviants possess understandings that the pious are unaware of. "بَصُرتُ بما لم یبصروا"

5- The designers of deviation exploit the ignorance of the people. "بمالم یبصروا"

6- The leaders of falsehood misuse even sacred things to mislead people. "من أثر الرّسول" (The policy of religion against religion)

7- If skill is accompanied by carnal desires, it poses the greatest danger. "سَوّلتْ لى نفسى" (My soul prompted me)

8- Unless a person is internally deceived by his own soul, he cannot deceive others. "سوّلت لى نفسى"

Ayahs

قَالَ فَٱذْهَبْ فَإِنَّ لَكَ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ أَن تَقُولَ لَا مِسَاسَ ۖ وَإِنَّ لَكَ مَوْعِدًا لَّن تُخْلَفَهُۥ ۖ وَٱنظُرْ إِلَىٰٓ إِلَـٰهِكَ ٱلَّذِى ظَلْتَ عَلَيْهِ عَاكِفًا ۖ لَّنُحَرِّقَنَّهُۥ ثُمَّ لَنَنسِفَنَّهُۥ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ نَسْفًا (۹۷)
He said, ‘Begone! It shall be your [lot] throughout life to say, ‘‘Do not touch me!’’ Indeed, there is a tryst for you which you will not fail to keep! Now look at your god to whom you went on clinging. We will burn it down and then scatter it[s ashes] into the sea. (97)
إِنَّمَآ إِلَـٰهُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ عِلْمًا (۹۸)
Indeed your God is Allah. There is no god except Him. He embraces all things in [His] knowledge.’ (98)

Key Points

A narration is mentioned in the commentaries Majma‘ al-Bayan and Safi that Prophet Moses عليه السلام intended to kill the Samiri, but Allah revealed to him that since the Samiri was a generous man, He should refrain from killing him. Therefore, Moses عليه السلام expelled him from the midst of the Children of Israel with the phrase, "فاذهب" (Go away). The word "لامساس" (Lamisas) means being afflicted with a disease such that no one touches him under any circumstances. Eventually, the Samiri was afflicted with a psychological illness where he would flee from people, and whenever anyone approached him, he would shout: "لامساس" (Do not touch), "دور شو، دور شو" (Go away, go away).(111) Prophet Moses عليه السلام decreed several punishments for the Samiri: A: Expulsion ("فاذهب"), B: Curse ("لامساس"), C: Threat of punishment in the Hereafter ("لك موعداً"), D: Burning the calf ("لنحرّقنّه").

111) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- After the crime is proven, the criminal must be punished. "سَولّتْ لى نفسى - فاذهب"

2- One stage of Nahy ‘anil Munkar (forbidding the wrong) is the expulsion of criminals and disbelievers. "فاذهب"

3- Cultural corruptors must be expelled from society. Freedom of thought does not mean giving deviants free rein to mislead others. "فاذهب"

4- Prophets, through divine knowledge of the unseen (‘Ilm al-Ghayb), inform about the future of individuals. "أن تقول لامساس"

5- Some diseases are divine wrath. "لامساس"

6- For promoters of false ideas, worldly punishment is not a means of reducing the penalties in the Hereafter. "لامساس و أنّ لك موعداً"

7- It might be possible to escape worldly punishments, but there is no escape from the torment of the Hereafter and Allah’s wrath therein. "لك موعداً لن تخلفه"

8- The worst type of crime is the criminal's insistence on the crime. "ظلت علیه عاكفاً" (The Samiri himself was also worshipping his calf.)

9- The tools of sin and the remnants of deviation must be destroyed.(112) "لنحرّقنّه"

10- The eradication of the remnants of disbelief (Kufr) and polytheism (Shirk) must be done publicly and in the presence of the people. "لنحرّقنّه" (We shall surely burn it) and He did not say: "لاُحرّقنّه" (I shall surely burn it).

11- Protecting people's thoughts is more important than preserving gold. Sometimes, valuable objects must be sacrificed to create a movement and combat evil (Munkar). "لنحرّقنّه"

12- Religious zeal and decisiveness against deviation are prerequisites for leadership. "لنحرّقنّه ثم لننسفنّه" (Burning the gold and scattering it into the sea was Moses' definitive decision.)

13- It must be shown that perishable things are not worthy of worship. "لنحرّقنّه ثم لننسفنّه"

14- Whenever you eradicate falsehood, present the truth in its place. "انّما الهكم اللّه"

15- The God worthy of worship is He Who has comprehensive knowledge of everything. "وسع كلّ شى ء علما"

112) Just as the Prophet of Islam ordered that the Mosque of Dissent (Masjid al-Dirar) first be burned, its remainder demolished, and the site turned into a dumping ground for the people of Medina. Tafsir Namuneh.

Ayah

كَذَٰلِكَ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ مَا قَدْ سَبَقَ ۚ وَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَـٰكَ مِن لَّدُنَّا ذِكْرًا (۹۹)
Thus do We relate to you some accounts of what is past. Certainly, We have given you a Reminder from Ourselves. (99)

Key Points

There are many blessings in relating the history of the Prophets, including: A: increasing knowledge and awareness, B: gaining insight and finding the path to felicity, C: admonishing and reminding people, D: giving glad tidings and encouragement to the believers.

Messages

1- Relating history is one of the educational methods of the Quran. «كذلك نقصّ»

2- The stories of the Quran are the best and most truthful stories, because their Speaker is Allah, their addressee is the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, and their events are real. «نقصّ علیك»

3- When stating history, refrain from relating details whose knowledge or lack thereof plays no role in the objective. «من انباء»

4- Not every history is worth stating; important news is worthy of narration.(113) «انباء»

5- History is a means of remembrance, not a factor for entertainment. «انباء ما قد سبق - ذكرا»

6- The Quran is an important remembrance and reminder. («ذكراً» with tanwin is a sign of greatness.)

7- The source of Quranic history and stories is Divine revelation, not the sayings of this person or that. «لدنّا» 113) «انباء» is the plural of naba’, which refers to important and beneficial news.

Ayahs

مَّنْ أَعْرَضَ عَنْهُ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَحْمِلُ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ وِزْرًا (۱٠٠)
Whoever disregards it shall bear a burden [of punishment] on the Day of Resurrection, (100)
خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهِ ۖ وَسَآءَ لَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ حِمْلًا (۱٠۱)
remaining in it [forever]. Evil is their burden on the Day of Resurrection (101)
يَوْمَ يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ ۚ وَنَحْشُرُ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ يَوْمَئِذٍ زُرْقًا (۱٠۲)
—the day the Trumpet will be blown; on that day We shall muster the guilty with blind eyes. (102)
يَتَخَـٰفَتُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ إِن لَّبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا عَشْرًا (۱٠۳)
They will whisper to one another: ‘You have stayed only for ten [days].’ (103)

Key Points

The words ḥiml (حِمل) and ḥaml (حَمل) have the same meaning linguistically. However, ḥiml refers to an external load, while ḥaml refers to an internal load, such as a baby in the mother's womb. Zurq (زُرق) is a color resulting from the mixture of white and black, like gray, and perhaps it is a metaphor for blindness, fear, or paleness. The word ṣūr (صُور) either means a horn (trumpet) or is the plural of ṣūrah (form/image), referring to the blowing into the forms (bodies) of the dead for resurrection. The Glorious Qur'an refers to two blasts (نفخه): in the first blast, all creatures die, and in the second blast, humans are brought back to life for accountability. "وَنُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ فَصَعِقَ مَن فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَن فِي الْأَرْضِ إِلَّا مَن شَاءَ اللَّهُ ثُمَّ نُفِخَ فِيهِ أُخْرَىٰ فَإِذَا هُمْ قِيَامٌ يَنظُرُونَ" (114) And the intent of "يُنفَخُ فِي الصُّورِ" (the Trumpet will be blown) in this verse, based on the context of the phrase "وَنَحْشُرُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ" (and We will gather the criminals), is the second blast on the Day of Resurrection.

114) Az-Zumar, 68.

Messages

1- Humans are free in choosing their path; they can accept the truth or they can turn away. (A‘raḍa) 2- The consequence of turning away from the remembrance of Allah is not limited only to a miserable life in this world, "مَنۡ أَعۡرَضَ فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ مَعِيشَةٗ ضَنكٗا" (Whoever turns away, indeed, for him is a restricted life) (115), but also brings the misfortune of the Hereafter. "مَنۡ أَعۡرَضَ... يَحۡمِلُ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ وِزۡرٗا" (Whoever turns away... will carry a burden on the Day of Resurrection). 3- The extent of the danger and the heaviness of the burden on the Day of Resurrection is not clear to anyone. (Wizran [وزراً] is indefinite). 4- On the Day of Resurrection, the criminal will either be gathered blind and black-faced, or afflicted with blue eyes due to fear, terror, and staring. (Zurqan) 5- The duration of the world and the intermediate realm (Barzakh) is very insignificant compared to the Hereafter, amounting to only a few days. "إِن لَّبِثۡتُمۡ إِلَّا عَشۡرٗا" (You remained not except for ten [days]).

115) Taha, 124.

Ayah

نَّحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ إِذْ يَقُولُ أَمْثَلُهُمْ طَرِيقَةً إِن لَّبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا يَوْمًا (۱٠٤)
We know best what they will say, when the smartest of them in approach will say, ‘You stayed only a day!’ (104)

Key Points

The grandeur of the Day of Judgment is such that when people are present there, they reckon their life in this world as half a day, or one day, or at most ten days. The Quran expresses this through the words of various individuals and groups: A: Ten days. «ان لبثتم الاّ عشرا» B: One day. «ان لبثتم الاّ یوما» C: Part of a day. «لم یلبثوا الاّ عشیّة او ضحیها»(116), «لبثت یوماً او بعض یوم»(117) D: Short moments. «ما لبثوا غیر ساعة»(118) E: A short time. «ان لبثتم الاّ قلیلا»(119) It seems that this difference in opinion regarding the duration of worldly life depends on the degree of insight and understanding of the individuals. Just as in this Ayah, those whom the Quran refers to as «امثلهم طریقة» (the most exemplary in conduct) count the duration of worldly life as one day, while in the previous Ayah, the sinners count its duration as ten days.
