The Quran expresses this through the words of various individuals and groups: A: Ten days. «ان لبثتم الاّ عشرا» B: One day. «ان لبثتم الاّ یوما» C: Part of a day. «لم یلبثوا الاّ عشیّة او ضحیها»(116), «لبثت یوماً او بعض یوم»(117) D: Short moments. «ما لبثوا غیر ساعة»(118) E: A short time. «ان لبثتم الاّ قلیلا»(119) It seems that this difference in opinion regarding the duration of worldly life depends on the degree of insight and understanding of the individuals. Just as in this Ayah, those whom the Quran refers to as «امثلهم طریقة» (the most exemplary in conduct) count the duration of worldly life as one day, while in the previous Ayah, the sinners count its duration as ten days.

Messages

  1. Not all criminals (sinners) are at the same level on the Day of Judgment. «امثلهم طریقة»
  2. Whoever is wiser sees the world as smaller (shorter). «یقول امثلهم طریقة اِن لبثتم الاّ یوما»

Ayahs

وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلْجِبَالِ فَقُلْ يَنسِفُهَا رَبِّى نَسْفًا (۱٠٥)
They question you concerning the mountains. Say, ‘My Lord will scatter them [like dust].’ (105)
فَيَذَرُهَا قَاعًا صَفْصَفًا (۱٠٦)
Then He will leave it a level plain. (106)
لَّا تَرَىٰ فِيهَا عِوَجًا وَلَآ أَمْتًا (۱٠۷)
You will not see any crookedness or unevenness in it. (107)

Key Points

The word nasf (نَسْف) means uprooting, eradication, and destruction. Qā‘ (قاع) means a flat land. Ṣafṣaf (صَفْصَف) means a flat land such that all its parts are in one line or row. The words ‘iwaj (عِوَج) and amt (اَمْت) mean depression and elevation (or unevenness).

Messages

1- People have questions about the Day of Judgment, and the Prophet must answer them. «یسئلونك... قل ینسفها ربّى»

2- The system governing nature is not permanent. «ینسفها ربّى»

3- The disintegration of mountains and the creation of the Day of Judgment is a manifestation of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ینسفها ربّى نسفاً»

Ayah

يَوْمَئِذٍ يَتَّبِعُونَ ٱلدَّاعِىَ لَا عِوَجَ لَهُۥ ۖ وَخَشَعَتِ ٱلْأَصْوَاتُ لِلرَّحْمَـٰنِ فَلَا تَسْمَعُ إِلَّا هَمْسًا (۱٠۸)
On that day they will follow the summoner without zigzagging. Their voices will be muted before the All-beneficent, and you will hear nothing but a murmur. (108)

Messages

1- If people in the world turned away from the Divine callers, on the Day of Resurrection, they will have no choice but to follow. «یتّبعون الدّاعى»

2- The Resurrection is the day of the manifestation and appearance of Allah's Mercy (Rahmaniyyah), and His Mercy is the hope and refuge of all people. «خشعت الاصوات للرحمن»

Ayah

يَوْمَئِذٍ لَّا تَنفَعُ ٱلشَّفَـٰعَةُ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ وَرَضِىَ لَهُۥ قَوْلًا (۱٠۹)
Intercession will not avail that day except from him whom the All-beneficent allows and approves of his word. (109)

Key Points

Since the denial of intercession (Shafa'ah) contradicts the Quran and narrations and causes despair for sinful believers, and conversely, the acceptance of unconditional intercession leads to the audacity of wrongdoers and violates Divine Justice, the Holy Quran has therefore established rules, regulations, and accountability for intercession. From the perspective of the Quran, intercession is a window of hope for sinners and a means for them to connect with the friends of Allah (Awliya Allah) and follow them. Intercession is by Allah's permission (Idhn). Those who benefit from intercession are only those who remain within the sphere of Monotheism (Tawhid), possess sound logic and true beliefs, and whose speech has been accepted by Allah. That is, their declaration of faith (Shahadatayn) must not be situational, seasonal, coerced, superficial, based on mockery, or hypocrisy. In this case, if they have shortcomings in their actions, they will receive favor through intercession. For this reason, the Quran invalidates the intercession of idols for idolaters. Furthermore, intercession understood as the sacrifice of Prophet Jesus عليه السلام to cleanse the sins of His followers, or the martyrdom of Imam Husayn عليه السلام solely for the sake of interceding for His followers, is unacceptable. Although Imam Husayn عليه السلام is one of the great intercessors on the Day of Judgment, it must be understood that His goal in martyrdom was not merely to intercede for His supporters. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: The intercession of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله is only for those whose actions and speech are satisfactory (to Allah), and who lived and died upon the love (Mawaddah) of the Prophet's Household علیهم السلام.(120)

120) Ta’wil al-Ayat, 304.

Messages

1- Intercession is a lofty status enjoyed by specific individuals. «مَن أذن له الرّحمن و رضى له قولا»

2- Intercession is not an interference with Allah's Will, nor is it a deviation from Divine Wisdom, Justice, calculations, or traditions (Sunan); rather, it is based on Allah's desire. «أذن... رضى»

Ayahs

يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِهِۦ عِلْمًا (۱۱٠)
He knows what is before them and behind them, though they do not comprehend Him in their knowledge. (110)
۞ وَعَنَتِ ٱلْوُجُوهُ لِلْحَىِّ ٱلْقَيُّومِ ۖ وَقَدْ خَابَ مَنْ حَمَلَ ظُلْمًا (۱۱۱)
All faces shall be humbled before the Living One, the All-sustainer, and those who bear [the burden of] wrongdoing will fail. (111)

Key Points

The word 'anat (عَنَت) is derived from 'anwah (عَنوة), meaning humiliation in the face of force and dominance. Qayyūm (قیّوم) refers to one who is self-subsistent, the Preserver of all things, and who has granted the means of subsistence to everything. The word khāba (خاب) is derived from khaybah (خیبة), meaning the loss of what is sought.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, the reckoning is extremely precise, because the Reckoner knows everything well, and there is no way to misuse intercession (shafā'ah). «یعلم ما بین ایدیهم و ...»

2- Allah’s comprehensive knowledge regarding the past and future deeds of people is uniform. «یعلم مابین ایدیهم و ما خلفهم»

3- The finite can never encompass the Infinite. «لا یحیطون به» Neither His Essence, nor His Attributes, nor His creations, nor His power and deeds.

4- The spiritual states of man manifest first and foremost upon his face. «عنت الوجوه»

5- The ultimate consequence of oppression (zulm) is deprivation from Divine Mercy. «و قد خاب من حمل ظلما»

6- Many acts of oppression can be compensated for and erased in this world through repentance (tawbah) and seeking forgiveness from the people. Wretched is he who carries his oppression until the Day of Judgment. «خاب من حمل ظلما»

Ayah

وَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَا يَخَافُ ظُلْمًا وَلَا هَضْمًا (۱۱۲)
But whoever does righteous deeds, being faithful, will neither fear any injustice or disparagement. (112)

Key Points

The word hadhm (هَضم) means reduction or decrease. Perhaps the absorption of food in the body is called hadhm because the food apparently decreases and its residue remains. (121)

It is understood from the Quran and narrations that human deeds and spiritual states have a reciprocal effect on one another. Therefore, whenever a good deed originates from a hypocrite, polytheist, show-off (riya'kar), etc., who possesses a corrupt disposition, it will not be accepted. Similarly, if a disbeliever performs a worthy and good act, such as a beneficial invention or discovery, while having understood the truth and knowingly insisting on disbelief, his good deed will not be accepted. This is like pouring sweet and pleasant syrup into a dirty and contaminated container, which naturally makes it unacceptable.

In the last ten verses, a brief portrait of the establishment of the Day of Resurrection has been mentioned: A: The Trumpet will be blown, and the dead will be brought back to life. «یوم ینفخ فى الصور» B: The criminals will be gathered. «نحشر المجرمین» C: The mountains will be scattered. «ینسفها ربّى نسفا» D: Everyone will be obedient to the call of the Divine Caller. «یتّبعون الداعى» E: Intercession is not effective without Allah's permission. «لاتنفع الشفاعه» F: Allah, with His comprehensive knowledge, will settle all accounts. «یعلم مابین ایدیهم و...» G: All faces will be humbled before the Divine Decree. «عنت الوجوه» H: The oppressors are disappointed. «خاب مَن حمل ظلما» I: The righteous believers will live in peace. «لایخاف ظلما و لاهضما». (122)

121) Tafsir Namuneh. 122) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Although performing all worthy deeds is not possible, one must perform righteous deeds according to one's capacity and ability. «مَن یعمل من الصالحات»

2- Not only great deeds, but even an atom of righteous work will not go unrewarded. «من الصالحات»

3- The condition for the acceptance of righteous deeds is faith in Allah. «و هو مؤمن»

4- Faith is not separate from action; faith without action is like a fruitless tree, and action without faith is like a rootless tree. «و هو مؤمن»

5- The spiritual peace and security of the believers on the Day of Resurrection depend on their righteous deeds. «من یعمل من الصالحات...فلا یخاف ظلما»

6- On the Day of Resurrection, neither the deed itself nor the reward will be destroyed, resulting in oppression «لا یخاف ظلما», nor will the reward be reduced. «و لا هضما»

7- Although the reward is a Divine favor (Tafaddul) and not a human entitlement, Allah does not fall short or reduce anyone's reward. «لا یخاف ظلما و لاهضما»

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ قُرْءَانًا عَرَبِيًّا وَصَرَّفْنَا فِيهِ مِنَ ٱلْوَعِيدِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ أَوْ يُحْدِثُ لَهُمْ ذِكْرًا (۱۱۳)
Thus We have sent it down as an Arabic Quran and We have paraphrased the warnings in it variously so that they may be Godwary, or it may prompt them to remembrance. (113)

Key Points

«كذلك انزلناه» (Thus have We sent it down) means that by describing the scenes of the Day of Judgment (in the previous verses) and the corresponding rewards and punishments, We sent down the Qur'an in this manner as a warning. The word «عربى» (Arabiyyan) linguistically means clear (or eloquent), and it is probable that it means pertaining to the Arabic language. The word «صرّفنا» (Sarrafnā) means various explanations of a single reality; sometimes through allusion, sometimes through argumentation, sometimes through examples and parables, and sometimes through recounting history.

Messages

1- The sacredness of the law of revelation («انزلنا»), the clarity of its objectives («عربیّا»), the variety and diversity of its explanations («صرفّنا»), and its nature as a warning («من الوعید») are the foundations for piety and acceptance of advice. «لعلّهم یتّقون او یحدث لهم ذكرا»

2- All people need revelation, but some need it as a warning (for prevention) and others need it as a reminder (for reinforcement). «یتّقون، ذكرا»

3- Warning is not without effect; either it has a profound impact, «یتّقون» (they become righteous), or at the very least, it serves as a reminder. «ذكرا» (a remembrance). Either it yields results, «یتّقون», or it completes the argument (establishes proof). «ذكرا»

Ayah

فَتَعَـٰلَى ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۗ وَلَا تَعْجَلْ بِٱلْقُرْءَانِ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يُقْضَىٰٓ إِلَيْكَ وَحْيُهُۥ ۖ وَقُل رَّبِّ زِدْنِى عِلْمًا (۱۱٤)
So exalted is Allah, the True Sovereign. Do not hasten with the Quran before its revelation is completed for you, and say, ‘My Lord! Increase me in knowledge.’ (114)

Key Points

It is understood from the phrase "لاتعجل بالقرآن" (123) that the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله knew the entire collection of the Quranic verses and would recite them beforehand. This is a clear proof that the Glorious Quran was revealed to the Prophet twice: once instantaneously (daf'i) and another time gradually (tadriji). What the Prophet had received during the instantaneous revelation on the Night of Qadr (Laylat al-Qadr), He would recite before the revelation arrived during the gradual descent (124).

There is a fundamental difference between haste and impatience (shatabzadegi wa ajalah) on the one hand, and speed and precedence (sur'at wa sabqat), which are praised and commanded in the Quran using the phrases "سارعوا" (125) and "سابقوا" (126), on the other hand. The virtue of speed and precedence lies where all matters have been calculated and organized; thus, one should not lose the opportunity or time. However, haste and impatience occur when the time for execution has not yet arrived, or when there is a need for completion and review; therefore, one must pause.

The source of haste and impatience is sometimes negative matters such as lack of patience, arrogance, or showing off, which are certainly reprehensible traits and far removed from the sacred nature of the Prophet. Other times, it is due to intense love and desire to receive the message, and concern for preserving something. In this case, it is considered a good thing. The Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله haste regarding the revelation was due to this reason: a loving reception, excitement, and anxiety to preserve the revelation.

Allah entrusted Prophet Moses عليه السلام to Prophet Khidr for instruction. Therefore, Moses عليه السلام sought permission from him, saying: "هل اتّبعك على أن تعلّمن ممّا علّمت رشدا" (127) (May I follow you so that you may teach me some of the guidance you have been taught?) However, the sacred Divine Essence took charge of the education and instruction of the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and told Him: "قل ربّ زدنى علما" Say: My Lord! Increase me in knowledge.

The first Teacher is Allah, the All-Knowing (Allam) and the Omniscient (Alim): "علّم آدم الاسماء" (128), "الرّحمن، علّم القرآن، علّمه البیان" (129). Therefore, let us seek knowledge from Him. "ربّ زدنى علماً" It is narrated in a hadith that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: If a day passes over me and my knowledge is not increased therein, that day is not blessed for me (130).

(123) A similar phrase also appears in Surah Al-Qiyamah, Ayah 18: "لاتحرّك به لسانك لتعجل به" (124) Tafsir al-Mizan. (125) Al ‘Imran, 133. (126) Al-Hadid, 21. (127) Al-Kahf, 66. (128) Al-Baqarah, 31. (129) Ar-Rahman, 1-3. (130) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Creation is not aimless, nor is man left unrestrained, because futility and aimlessness are not befitting of Allah. "فتعالى اللّه"

2- We must consider the sacred Divine Essence superior to every thought and idea, and His actions pure and exalted above every false matter. "فتعالى اللّه"

3- Wise, true, absolute, and unique sovereignty belongs to Allah. "الملك الحق"

4- The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله was passionately eager to hear and receive the revelation. "لاتعجل بالقرآن"

5- Learning anything has its own corresponding etiquette. "لا تعجل بالقرآن"

6- The prohibitions and admonishments in the Quran directed at the Prophet are a sign of the Prophet's sincerity; otherwise, no one would subject himself to prohibition and admonishment. "لاتعجل بالقرآن"

7- In stating Divine laws, temporal conditions must be considered. "لاتعجل... قبل أن یقضى"

8- Let us seek knowledge from the True Knower. "ربّ زدنى علما"

9- There is no limit to knowledge and learning; graduation (in the sense of completion) has no meaning. "زدنى علما"

10- True knowledge is the Glorious Quran. "لا تعجل بالقرآن - و قل ربّ زدنى علما"

11- If we prohibit someone from something, we should also show him the correct and true path in return. "لا تعجل - قل ربّ زدنى علما"

12- Seeking increase in perfections is a virtue. "ربّ زدنى علما"

13- Knowledge is the means for human growth. "ربّ زدنى علما"

14- Knowledge is valuable and credible only if, along with its acquisition, human capacity also increases and grows. "زدنى علما" (Increase me in knowledge), not "زد علمى" (Increase my knowledge).

15- Although Allah can bestow favor without prayer, He asks His Prophet to pray. "قل ربّ زدنى علما"

16- In prayer, let us seek help through the word Rabb (Lord) and the Lordship of Allah. "ربّ زدنى علما"

17- Except for Allah, the knowledge of everyone, even the Prophet, is limited. "ربّ زدنى علما"

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ عَهِدْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ ءَادَمَ مِن قَبْلُ فَنَسِىَ وَلَمْ نَجِدْ لَهُۥ عَزْمًا (۱۱٥)
Certainly We had enjoined Adam earlier; but he forgot, and We did not find any resoluteness in him. (115)
وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ ٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِـَٔادَمَ فَسَجَدُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ إِبْلِيسَ أَبَىٰ (۱۱٦)
When We said to the angels, ‘Prostrate before Adam,’ they prostrated, but not Iblis: he refused. (116)

Key Points

This is the sixth time, since the beginning of the Qur'an until now, that we encounter the story of Adam and Iblis. Previously, references to this matter were made in Surahs Al-Baqarah, Al-A'raf, Al-Hijr, Al-Isra', and Al-Kahf. (131) The intent of the covenant and promise is the command not to eat from the specific tree. The intent of nisyan (forgetting) is the lack of precision in executing the command; otherwise, absolute forgetfulness does not warrant reproach or criticism. The meaning of 'azm (resolve) is firm will in the face of Iblis's temptations. Imam Kadhim عليه السلام said: The command to prostrate and Iblis's disobedience is a form of consolation and reassurance for the Prophet of Islam, meaning: if your command is not followed, do not worry, for Iblis did not obey My command either. (132)

(131) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr al-Razi. (132) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 412.

Messages

1- Allah has not abandoned humanity to its own devices. «عهدنا الى آدم»

2- Man is a being naturally exposed to the danger of excess and negligence, unless Allah protects him. «لا تعجل - فنسى»

3- Resolve ('azm) to fulfill divine duties is necessary. «لم نجد له عزما»

4- Among the important factors leading to human deviation are: first, heedlessness regarding the dignity and status of humanity, and second, heedlessness regarding the internal and external enemy, against which the Qur'an has warned repeatedly. «و اذقلنا»

5- The prostration of the angels before Adam, commanded by Allah, was due to Adam's high dignities and a sign of his superiority over them; otherwise, Iblis would not have said: «انَا خیر منه» (I am better than him). «اسجدوا لآدم»

6- The angels do not hesitate for a moment in carrying out their divine mission. «فسجدوا»

Ayahs

فَقُلْنَا يَـٰٓـَٔادَمُ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا عَدُوٌّ لَّكَ وَلِزَوْجِكَ فَلَا يُخْرِجَنَّكُمَا مِنَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ فَتَشْقَىٰٓ (۱۱۷)
We said, ‘O Adam! This is indeed an enemy of yours and your mate’s. So do not let him expel you from paradise, or you will be miserable. (117)
إِنَّ لَكَ أَلَّا تَجُوعَ فِيهَا وَلَا تَعْرَىٰ (۱۱۸)
You will neither be hungry in it nor naked. (118)
وَأَنَّكَ لَا تَظْمَؤُا۟ فِيهَا وَلَا تَضْحَىٰ (۱۱۹)
You will neither be thirsty in it, nor suffer from [the heat of] the sun.’ (119)

Key Points

Although Satan is introduced here only as the enemy of Adam and Eve, in another place, a warning is given about his enmity towards all human beings. «عدوٌ لكم»(133) The meaning of «تشقى» (tashqā) here is the hardships of material life, which is inferred from the subsequent verses where He says: In Paradise, there is no hunger, nakedness, or thirst, meaning that if you leave Paradise, you will be afflicted with hardship.

(133) Nisa, 92.

Messages

1- One of the greatest Divine favors upon His servants is the introduction of the danger posed by Satan. «هذا عدوٌ»

2- The war and enmity between truth and falsehood have existed since the beginning of human creation. «هذا عدوٌ لك و لزوجك»

3- Both man and woman are within the range of the enemy's propaganda and Satan's temptations. «عدوٌ لك و لزوجك»

4- The Divine tradition is based on completing the argument (Hujjah) with His servants. «هذا عدوٌ لك و لزوجك»

5 - Life in this world is accompanied by hardship. «فتشقى»

6- The primary needs and desires of man manifest in four things: hunger and thirst, which are internal hardships; and nakedness and homelessness, which are external hardships. All four are mentioned in the verse. «الاّ تجوع، لاتعرى، لاتظمأ لاتضحى»

Ayahs

فَوَسْوَسَ إِلَيْهِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ قَالَ يَـٰٓـَٔادَمُ هَلْ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَىٰ شَجَرَةِ ٱلْخُلْدِ وَمُلْكٍ لَّا يَبْلَىٰ (۱۲٠)
Then Satan tempted him. He said, ‘O Adam! Shall I show you the tree of immortality, and an imperishable kingdom?’ (120)
فَأَكَلَا مِنْهَا فَبَدَتْ لَهُمَا سَوْءَٰتُهُمَا وَطَفِقَا يَخْصِفَانِ عَلَيْهِمَا مِن وَرَقِ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۚ وَعَصَىٰٓ ءَادَمُ رَبَّهُۥ فَغَوَىٰ (۱۲۱)
So they both ate of it, and their nakedness became evident to them, and they began to stitch over themselves with the leaves of paradise. Adam disobeyed his Lord, and went amiss. (121)

Key Points

Satan (Shaytan) is another name for Iblis. «فسجدوا الا ابلیس... فوسوس الیه الشیطان» Adam's temptation occurred before he attained the rank of prophethood. (134)

Sayyid Ibn Tawus (قدس سره) states in a subtle explanation: Satan initially approached Adam with the slogan of guidance and direction, «هل ادلّك» (Shall I guide you?), and then he misled and tempted him, «فدلاّهما بغرور» (So he misled them with deception) (135). Woe then to us, for Satan comes to us from the very beginning intending to deceive and mislead us. «فبعزّتك لاغوینّهم اجمعین» (136)

The word ghawa (غَوى) is derived from ghayy (غَىّ), which is the opposite of rushd (guidance/maturity), meaning a path that prevents a person from reaching their destination. Allah said concerning Adam: «فغوى» (So he went astray), but concerning the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, He says: «ما ضلّ صاحبكم و ما غوى» (137)

Satan's temptation does not negate human free will. Therefore, after being tempted, a person is subject to self-reproach and the blame of others. If temptation negated human free will, no one would criticize them.

Several possibilities have been suggested regarding the sin of Prophet Adam concerning the phrase «عصى آدم ربّه فغوى» (Adam disobeyed his Lord, so he went astray):

A: Adam did not commit a sin, but rather committed a tark al-awla (abandonment of the better course), and ghawa (غوى) means he failed to achieve rushd (maturity), not that he was misguided.

B: The prohibition given to Adam regarding the forbidden tree was a prohibitive guidance (nahy irshadi), not a mandatory prohibition (nahy mawlawi). (138)

C: Isyan (عصیان - disobedience) is used both for abandoning an obligation (wajib) and abandoning a recommended act (mustahabb). (139)

However, none of these possibilities are acceptable because:

  1. This Ayah and similar Ayahs explicitly state Adam's disobedience (isyan).
  2. We have no proof that the prohibition was merely advisory (irshadi).
  3. The claim that there was no mandatory religious ruling (hukm taklifi) at that time is incorrect, because in that case, we would have to say that Satan also did not commit a violation or a sin.
  4. Does repentance (tawbah) from a prohibited act make sense if the prohibition was merely advisory (irshadi)?
  5. The term isyan (عصیان) in the Quran is used only in the sense of sin.

Therefore, it is better to turn to the infallible Imam, where Ma’mun asked Imam al-Ridha (عليه السلام): If the Prophets are infallible (ma'sum), then what is the meaning of the Ayah «و عصى آدم ربّه فغوى»? The Imam (عليه السلام) replied: «كان ذلك من آدم قبل النبوة... من الذنوب الّتى تجوز على الانبیاء قبل نزول الوحى علیهم...» (140) Meaning, the disobedience of Adam occurred before his prophethood, and it was a violation whose commission does not contradict the status of prophethood.

134) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr Razi. 135) Al-A'raf, 22. 136) Sad, 82. 137) An-Najm, 2. 138) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 139) Tafsir Namuneh. 140) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 11, p. 164; Uyun Akhbar al-Ridha, Vol. 1, p. 195.

Messages

  1. The path of Satan's infiltration is through human desires. «هل ادلّك على شجرة الخلد» (Satan was aware of human needs and desires, thus he deceived Adam through the desire for immortality. Indeed, long and drawn-out hopes lead humans to sin.)
  2. Satans use beautiful and attractive names and titles to mislead people and achieve their sinister goals. «هل ادلّك على شجرة الخلد»
  3. Lying is Satan's tool for deceiving humans. «هل ادلّك على شجرة الخلد»
  4. If the friends of Allah (Awliya) commit a slip, they are immediately warned/punished. «فبدت»
  5. The manifestation of ugliness and nakedness was the first effect of Adam's disobedience. «فبدت لهما»
  6. Covering (modesty) is dignity and beauty for humans, while nakedness is baseness and ugliness. «سوءاتهما»
  7. An unlawful morsel causes the loss of shame and modesty and the manifestation of ugliness. «فاكلا... فبدت لهما سوءاتهما»
  8. Covering and concealing the private parts is inherent in the nature (fitrah) of all humans. «و طفقا یخصفان»
  9. Opposition to the command of Allah is opposition to one's own growth and development. «عصى آدم ربّه»
  10. The consequences of sin are not the same for all who commit it. «فاكلا... عصى آدم» (Although both disobeyed and ate from the forbidden tree, the disobedience is attributed specifically to Adam.)
  11. Sin causes human deprivation/loss. «عصى... فغوى»

Ayahs

ثُمَّ ٱجْتَبَـٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ فَتَابَ عَلَيْهِ وَهَدَىٰ (۱۲۲)
Then his Lord chose him, and turned to him clemently, and guided him. (122)
قَالَ ٱهْبِطَا مِنْهَا جَمِيعًۢا ۖ بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ ۖ فَإِمَّا يَأْتِيَنَّكُم مِّنِّى هُدًى فَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ هُدَاىَ فَلَا يَضِلُّ وَلَا يَشْقَىٰ (۱۲۳)
He said, ‘Get down both of you from it, all together, being enemies of one another! Yet, should any guidance come to you from Me, those who follow My guidance will not go astray, nor will they be miserable. (123)

Key Points

Ijtabā’ (اجتباء) is derived from Jibāyah (جبایة), meaning collection, selection, and choosing. If Tāba (تاب) is used with the preposition ilā (الى), it signifies the repentance of man, like the phrase Tubtu ilayka (تبت الیك). But if it is placed alongside the preposition ‘alā (على), it is considered the repentance of Allah, as in the above Ayah, meaning Allah returned His favor upon the servant. Regarding who the addressees of ihbiṭā (اهبطا) are, it can be said that they are either Adam and Eve, or man and Satan, or Adam and his progeny. It is narrated in a Hadith from the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله that He said: No prophet wept as much as Prophet Adam علیه السلام (141) until he repented after his disobedience.

141) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr Razi.

Messages

1- When selecting individuals, pay attention to their pure nature and do not make one or two slips the criterion for rejection. Thumma ijtabāhu Rabbuhu fatāba ‘alayhi (ثم اجتباه ربّه فتاب علیه).

2- Adam's sin occurred before his appointment to the rank of prophethood. Thumma ijtabāhu (ثم اجتباه).

3- Time plays a role in selection. Thumma ijtabāhu (ثمّ اجتباه). (Thumma (ثمّ) indicates the passage of time).

4- Accepting repentance is among the affairs of Divine Lordship. Thumma ijtabāhu Rabbuhu fatāba ‘alayhi (ثمّ اجتباه ربّه فتاب علیه).

5- Instead of rejecting erring individuals, we should attract and guide them through forgiveness. Fatāba ‘alayhi wa hadā (فتاب علیه و هدى).

6- Repentance is the groundwork for guidance. Fatāba ‘alayhi wa hadā (فتاب علیه و هدى).

7- The acceptance of repentance does not prevent the manifestation of the consequential effects of sin. Fatāba ‘alayhi - ihbiṭā (فتاب علیه - اهبطا).

8- One sin can keep a generation in descent (hoboṭ). Ihbiṭā minhā jamī‘an (اهبطا منها جمیعاً).

9- The descent of mankind leads to the emergence of enmities. Ihbiṭā... ba‘ḍukum li-ba‘ḍin ‘aduww (اهبطا... بعضكم لبعض عدّو).

10- The first glad tidings given to Adam was the coming of Divine guidance, by following which the path to happiness and return to Paradise is opened. Ihbiṭā - fa-man ittaba‘a... (اهبطا - فمن اتبع ...).

11- The coming of the Prophets and Divine guidance is certain. (Ya’tiyannakum (یأتینّكم) contains the emphatic nūn).

12- In management and education, even if we issue a command of expulsion for certain interests, we must still provide the means for growth and reconstruction. Ihbiṭā - ya’tiyannakum minnī hudā... (اهبطا - یأتینّكم منّى هدى...).

13- No one but Allah can be the Guide (Hādī) and director of man. Ya’tiyannakum minnī hudā (یأتینّكم منّى هدى).

14- In the material world, the emergence of conflict and friction is certain; the important thing is that man does not lose the Divine path amidst this. Ba‘ḍukum li-ba‘ḍin ‘aduww... fa-man ittaba‘a hudāya... (بعضكم لبعض عدو... فمن اتّبع هداى...).

15- Humans are free in their choice. Fa-man ittaba‘a (فمن اتّبع).

16- The happiness and salvation of man lie in following Divine commands. Fa-man ittaba‘a hudāya falā yaḍillu wa lā yashqā (فمن اتّبع هداى فلایضلّ و لا یشقى).

Ayahs

وَمَنْ أَعْرَضَ عَن ذِكْرِى فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ مَعِيشَةً ضَنكًا وَنَحْشُرُهُۥ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ أَعْمَىٰ (۱۲٤)
But whoever disregards My remembrance, his shall be a wretched life, and We shall raise him blind on the Day of Resurrection.’ (124)
قَالَ رَبِّ لِمَ حَشَرْتَنِىٓ أَعْمَىٰ وَقَدْ كُنتُ بَصِيرًا (۱۲٥)
He will say, ‘My Lord! Why have You raised me blind, though I used to see?’ (125)

Key Points

The previous Ayah gave glad tidings to the followers of the truth, but this Ayah gives a warning and caution to those who turn away from the truth, so that fear and hope are presented side-by-side and are effective. In narrations, abandoning the path of the Wilayah (guardianship) of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام or neglecting the obligation of Hajj has been considered an instance of turning away from the remembrance of Allah. (142) The meaning of hardship and narrowness of life is not poverty; rather, many wealthy people live under pressure and constraint due to greed, fear, and anxiety. (143) Question: How can the blindness of the criminals be reconciled with the Ayah, «اقرء كتابك» (144), since reading the record of deeds requires eyes? Answer: Since there are multiple stations (stages) on the Day of Judgment, it is possible that they are sighted in one station and blind (اعمى) in another. Numerous narrations have considered the meaning of blindness on the Day of Judgment to be the inability to see the path of truth and goodness. (145) Imam Sajjad علیه السلام, after reciting the Ayah «مَن اعرض... ضنكاً», said: I swear by Allah! Indeed, the grave is either a garden from the gardens of Paradise or a pit from the pits of Hell. (146) Therefore, the scope of the narrow livelihood continues into the Barzakh (intermediate realm) as well.

142) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 143) Tafsir Namuneh. 144) Al-Isra, 14. 145) Man La Yahdhuruhu al-Faqih, Vol. 2, p. 447. 146) Bihar, Vol. 7, p. 104.

Messages

1- Humans are free in their choice. «من اعرض»

2- Human nature (Fitrah) is based on monotheism; the prophets and heavenly religions only remind people of it. «عن ذكرى» (147)

3- Distance from the remembrance of Allah causes anxiety, confusion, and regret, even if one possesses high financial means. «من اعرض ...»

4- A peaceful and sweet life is only possible in the light of the remembrance (Dhikr) of Allah. «من اعرض عن ذكرى»

5- Spirituality plays an important role even in material life. «ومن اعرض عن ذكرى فان له معیشة ضنكا»

6- Whoever closes the eye of his soul to the truths in this world will be resurrected blind on the Day of Judgment. «من اعرض - یوم القیامة اعمى»

7- The faithless person has a difficult life both in this world and is deprived of the blessing of sight in the Hereafter. «من اعرض - معیشة ضنكا - یوم القیامة اعمى»

147) Dhikr (remembrance) is the recalling of something that a person has forgotten.

Ayahs

قَالَ كَذَٰلِكَ أَتَتْكَ ءَايَـٰتُنَا فَنَسِيتَهَا ۖ وَكَذَٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمَ تُنسَىٰ (۱۲٦)
He will say: ‘So it is. Our signs came to you, but you forgot them, and so you will be forgotten today.’ (126)
وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى مَنْ أَسْرَفَ وَلَمْ يُؤْمِنۢ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِۦ ۚ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَشَدُّ وَأَبْقَىٰٓ (۱۲۷)
Thus do We requite those who transgress and do not believe in the signs of their Lord, and the punishment of the Hereafter is severer and more lasting. (127)

Key Points

One of the clear manifestations of the remembrance of Allah is prayer (Salat). Allah says: «اقم الصلوة لذكرى» (148) It is obvious that whoever remembers Allah, Allah will also remember him. This is the Divine promise: «اُذكرونى اَذكركم» (149) Consequently, those who forget Allah, Allah will also abandon them. «و كذلك الیوم تنسى» The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever recites the Quran but does not act upon it, Allah will resurrect him blind. That servant will say: Why have You resurrected me blind? The command will come: Take him to Hell. (150) Imam Sadiq علیه السلام, regarding the phrase «اتتك ایتنا», said: The infallible Imams are the signs (Ayat) of Allah, and whoever abandons them will be left in the Fire on the Day of Judgment. (151)

148) Taha, 14. 149) Al-Baqarah, 152. 150) Wasā’il, Vol. 6, p. 184. 151) Kāfī, Vol. 1, p. 436.

Messages

1- Divine punishments are just. «كذلك... نسیتها... تنسى»

2- If the source of forgetfulness (nisyān) is negligence, it will incur punishment; otherwise, forgetfulness that is involuntary carries no reproach. «فنسیتها»

3- Allah first completes the argument (proof), and only then does He inflict wrath and punishment. «اتتك ایاتنا... الیوم تنسى»

4- Extravagance (isrāf) and misuse of blessings pave the way for disbelief (kufr). «اسرف ولم یؤمن»

Ayahs

أَفَلَمْ يَهْدِ لَهُمْ كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْقُرُونِ يَمْشُونَ فِى مَسَـٰكِنِهِمْ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلنُّهَىٰ (۱۲۸)
Does it not dawn upon them how many generations We have destroyed before them, amid [the ruins of] whose dwellings they walk? There are indeed signs in this for those who have reason. (128)
وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَكَانَ لِزَامًا وَأَجَلٌ مُّسَمًّى (۱۲۹)
Were it not for a prior decree of your Lord and a specified time, [their doom] would have attended [their guilt]. (129)

Key Points

The word nuhā (نُهى) is the plural of nuhyah (نُهیه), meaning something that prohibits a person from indecencies. For this reason, the intellect ('aql) is called nuhyah, because it restrains man from desires. Therefore, true intellect, besides thinking and understanding, causes one to distance oneself from dangers and evils. Qarn (قَرن) means a people or nation living at the same time, and sometimes it refers to the time period itself. Lizām (لزام) means an inseparable and inevitable matter. The intent of kalimatu sabaqat (كلمة سبقت) is the statement that Allah made to Prophet Adam (عليه السلام) upon his descent: (152) «و لكم فى الارض مستقر و متاع الى حین» (153) The people of Mecca used to pass by the places of the people of 'Ād and Thamūd during their travels and would see their remaining ruins and remnants. In these verses, Allah warns them not to forget the fate of those nations and to take a lesson by reflecting and contemplating their remnants. (154)

152) Tafsir al-Mizan. 153) Al-Baqarah, 36. 154) Tafasir al-Mizan and Tibyan.

Messages

  1. History is the best teacher and the most truthful preacher. Whoever does not take a lesson from it deserves reproach. «أفلم يهد...»
  2. We must take divine threats seriously. «كم أهلكنا...»
  3. Historical transformations are in the hand of Allah. «أهلكنا...»
  4. Knowledge alone is not enough; taking a lesson is necessary. They see the events through their travels, yet they are heedless. «يمشون فى مساكنهم»
  5. Ruined and subjugated regions are a source of contemplation and reflection for every person of intellect. «آيات لأولى النّهى»
  6. When visiting ancient sites, one should not suffice merely with the recreational aspect. «آيات لأولى النّهى» Whoever does not take a lesson is senseless.
  7. Not everyone possesses the eye that sees lessons; taking lessons is specific to those who restrain their souls from impurities. «آيات لأولى النّهى»
  8. History possesses a philosophy and is based on the law of cause and effect. «كم أهلكنا - آيات لأولى النّهى» Yes, the destruction of previous nations was based on reasons, such that if those same matters exist today, the same destruction will occur.
  9. The divine tradition (Sunnah) is based on planning, scheduling, and granting respite; otherwise, the criminals would be destroyed immediately. «و لولا كلمة ...» (Every nation has a specific time.)
  10. Unbelieving oppressors should not be deceived by the delay of divine punishment. «كلمة سبقت من ربّك»
  11. Allah punishes the criminals after the completion of the argument (proof). «لولا كلمة سبقت من ربّك»
  12. The lifespan of humans is based on planning and scheduling. «أجلٌ مسّمى»

Ayah

فَٱصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَقَبْلَ غُرُوبِهَا ۖ وَمِنْ ءَانَآئِ ٱلَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْ وَأَطْرَافَ ٱلنَّهَارِ لَعَلَّكَ تَرْضَىٰ (۱۳٠)
So be patient with what they say, and celebrate the praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before the sunset, and glorify Him in watches of the night and at the day’s ends, that you may be pleased. (130)

Key Points

Allah has commanded the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله to observe patience 19 times in the Noble Qur'an. This Ayah, like the Ayah, «واستعینوا بالصبر والصلوة» (155), invites the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله to patience, praise, and glorification of the Lord. Although the apparent addressee of this Ayah is the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, it is obvious that the Ayah has a general application. According to some narrations (156) and the view of some commentators, this Ayah corresponds to the daily and nightly prayers, and the intent of «اطراف النهار» (the ends of the day) is the daily supererogatory prayers (Nawafil).

155) Baqarah, 45. 156) Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 444.

Messages

1- Attention to Allah's planning, the systematic nature of punishment and retribution, and attention to Divine traditions are prerequisites for patience, glorification (Tasbih), and praise (Tahmid). «فاصبر» (So be patient) (Considering the preceding verses).

2- Patience in matters must be commensurate with the difficulties. «فاصبر على ما یقولون» (So be patient over what they say).

3- The praise and glorification of Allah are means of strengthening morale against the obstinacy and propaganda of the enemy. «فاصبر - سبّح» (Be patient - Glorify).

4- The purification (Tanzih) of Allah is achieved through His praise and glorification. «سبّح بحمد» (Glorify [Him] with praise).

5- Cleansing from evils precedes adornment with good deeds. Glorification (Tasbih) precedes praise (Tahmid). «سبّح بحمد» (Glorify [Him] with praise).

6- The Qur'an uses natural tools and criteria to specify the time for performing duties. «قبل طلوع... وقبل غروبها» (before its rising... and before its setting).

7- The glorification and praise of Allah before sunrise and sunset have a special effect. «قبل طلوع... و قبل غروبها» (before its rising... and before its setting).

8- We should fill our time with the remembrance of Allah so that no room remains for other thoughts. «قبل طلوع - قبل غروب - آناء اللیل - اطراف النهار» (before rising - before setting - during the hours of the night - at the ends of the day).

9- Night is not solely for rest and sleep; a portion of it must be dedicated to worship. «و من آناء اللیل» (And during the hours of the night).

10- If the remembrance (Dhikr) of Allah is performed with other conditions, it will be fruitful. «لعلّك» (Perhaps you) (157).

11- The station of contentment (Rida) is the highest degree of perfection. «لعلّك ترضى» (that you may be satisfied).

(Perfection has degrees: First: Patience «فاصبر» (So be patient), Second: Purification of Allah «وسبّح... فسبّح» (And glorify... so glorify), Third: Gratitude and praise of the Lord «فسبّح بحمد ربّك» (So glorify your Lord with praise), Fourth: Contentment with the Divine decree. «لعلّك ترضى» (that you may be satisfied)).

157) The word «لعلّ» (perhaps) means that reaching this outcome is a percentage, not one hundred percent. This is because the remembrance of Allah alone is not sufficient; rather, effort, unity, sincerity, and other conditions must be combined for the result to be one hundred percent.

Ayah

وَلَا تَمُدَّنَّ عَيْنَيْكَ إِلَىٰ مَا مَتَّعْنَا بِهِۦٓ أَزْوَٰجًا مِّنْهُمْ زَهْرَةَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا لِنَفْتِنَهُمْ فِيهِ ۚ وَرِزْقُ رَبِّكَ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقَىٰ (۱۳۱)
Do not extend your glance toward what We have provided certain groups of them as a glitter of the life of this world, so that We may test them thereby. The provision of your Lord is better and more lasting. (131)

Key Points

It is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that He said: Whoever looks toward the hands of the people (depends on others for sustenance), his sorrow will be prolonged and his anger will remain. (158) Although the Noble Quran considers worldly wealth as khayr (goodness) (159), fadl (bounty) (160), and zinah (adornment) (161), and deems benefiting from it lawful and permissible, it nonetheless condemns intense attachment to it. This is because the life of this world, like a bud, has a short lifespan and vanishes with a single breeze.

158) Tafsir al-Mizan. 159) ان ترك خیراً» Baqarah, 180. 160) و ابتغوا من فضل اللّه» Jumu‘ah, 10. 161) المال و البنون زینة الحیوة الدنیا» Kahf, 46.

Messages

1- Attaining the station of satisfaction with Allah is possible only if you do not stare at the glitter of the world. (Indeed, whoever is attracted by material displays feels deprivation and discrimination, and thus is not satisfied with Allah.) «لعلّك ترضى، لا تمدّن عینیك»

2- Man naturally inclines toward material things, but he must restrain this inclination. «لاتمدّن»

3- Looking (gazing) is the prelude to attraction and attachment. «لا تمدّن عینیك»

A cry from the hand of both the eye and the heart, For whatever the eye sees, the heart remembers.

4- The leader of the Ummah (community) must not covet the wealth of others or pay attention to material things, nor should he be overwhelmed and captivated by the glittering world of others. «لا تمدّن عینیك»

5- Material displays can be dangerous even for the Prophets. «لاتمدّن عینیك»

6- Not all worldly possessions and means of enjoyment are considered Divine blessings (ni'mah). (The Quran uses) «متّعنا» (We gave them enjoyment), not «انعمنا» (We bestowed blessings).

7- Not all disbelievers are successful (or given enjoyment). «ازواجاً منهم» (pairs/groups among them)

8- The displays of worldly life are like a bud or a blossom that never fully becomes a flower. «زهرة الحیوة الدنیا»

9- The displays of worldly life are tools for testing human beings. «لنفتنهم فیه»

10- We should reflect upon what we possess ourselves, not what we see in the hands of others. «رزق ربّك خير»

11- Providing sustenance (rizq) is among the affairs of Allah’s Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «رزق ربّك»

12- Not all of a person's possessions constitute his sustenance (rizq). For there are many who have all kinds of facilities, but their own share (benefit) is small. «لاتمدنّ... متّعنا... رزق ربّك خير»

13- The criterion for the value of anything consists of two matters: first, being good (khayr), and second, being lasting (abqā). «خیر و ابقى»

Ayah

وَأْمُرْ أَهْلَكَ بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱصْطَبِرْ عَلَيْهَا ۖ لَا نَسْـَٔلُكَ رِزْقًا ۖ نَّحْنُ نَرْزُقُكَ ۗ وَٱلْعَـٰقِبَةُ لِلتَّقْوَىٰ (۱۳۲)
And bid your family to prayer and be steadfast in maintaining it. We do not ask any provision of you: it is We who provide for you, and to Godwariness belongs the ultimate outcome [in the Hereafter]. (132)

Key Points

It is mentioned in numerous narrations that after the revelation of this Ayah, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله would knock on the door of the house of Hadrat Ali and Fatimah علیهما السلام for several months, and standing at the threshold, He would say: «الصلوة» (The Prayer), and then recite the Ayah of Purification (Ayat al-Tathir). (162) Whenever the living situation of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله became difficult, He would recite this Ayah, and then command the members of His household to perform the prayer. (163) «اِصطَبر علیها» (Be patient and persistent in it) means be firm and steadfast both in performing the prayer and in commanding it.

162) Tafasir Kabir Fakhr Razi, Durr al-Manthur, and Majma' al-Bayan. 163) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Enjoining good (Amr bil Ma'ruf) is more effective when done by those who have not themselves been absorbed by materialism. «لا تمدّن... وامر اهلك»

2- The leader of the Islamic society should not neglect his family. «وامر اهلك بالصلوة»

3- A man is responsible for the intellectual and religious destiny of his family, and merely attending to their material affairs is not sufficient. «وأمر اهلك»

4- One of the most important spheres for enjoining good is a person's family and close relatives. «وأمر اهلك...» (The scope of 'family' (Ahl) is not limited to wife and children, but includes all members of the household.)

5- A preacher (Muballigh) must first focus on preaching to his own family. «وأمر اهلك»

6- The first stage of upbringing (Tarbiya) is the family nucleus. «وأمر اهلك»

7- The command for prayer, among other obligations, is the secret to insuring the members of the family. «وأمر اهلك بالصلوة»

8- To avoid attachment to the glitter and glamour of the world, we should take refuge in prayer. «لا تمدّن... وامر اهلك بالصلوة»

9- Prayer is one of the clear and high manifestations of good (Ma'ruf). «وأمر اهلك بالصلوة»

10- The subject of prayer holds special importance within the family; parents must insist upon it. (164) «وأمر اهلك بالصلوة واصطبر علیها»

11- Enjoining good requires continuity and steadfastness. «اصطبر علیها»

12- Allah gains no benefit from our worship; its fruit returns to us ourselves. «لانسئلك رزقا»

13- The recommendation of good deeds must be serious and accompanied by a statement of their consequences. «وامر اهلك... والعاقبة للتّقوى»

14- Prayer paves the way for chastity and piety (Taqwa). «وأمر اهلك بالصلوة... والعاقبة للتقوى»

15- A good outcome (Husn al-'Aqibah) is achieved through divine piety, not materialism. «والعاقبة للتقوى»

164) In studies conducted among students, adolescents, and youth, a high percentage of them cited their families' attention to prayer as the reason for their own attention to it.

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَا يَأْتِينَا بِـَٔايَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦٓ ۚ أَوَلَمْ تَأْتِهِم بَيِّنَةُ مَا فِى ٱلصُّحُفِ ٱلْأُولَىٰ (۱۳۳)
They say, ‘Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?’ Has there not come to them a clear proof in that which is in the former scriptures? (133)

Key Points

The polytheists expected that the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله would also bring a miracle similar to the staff of Moses and the splitting of the sea. But Allah says: Do you not know that the miracle of the Quran is superior to all previous miracles? Despite this, did they not show obstinacy against those very miracles?! The meaning of «صحف اولى» (the former scriptures) is the Torah, the Gospel, and the Psalms (Zabur).

Messages

1- Attention should not be paid to requests that are merely pretexts. «فاصبر على ما یقولون... و قالوا لولا یأتینا...» (It is the characteristic of obstinate and arrogant people that they disregard the existing clear miracles and proofs (the Quran) and demand another proof out of sheer stubbornness.)

2- The type of miracle is not important; the spirit of acceptance is necessary. «لولا یأتینا... او لم تأتهم»

Ayah

وَلَوْ أَنَّآ أَهْلَكْنَـٰهُم بِعَذَابٍ مِّن قَبْلِهِۦ لَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا لَوْلَآ أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَيْنَا رَسُولًا فَنَتَّبِعَ ءَايَـٰتِكَ مِن قَبْلِ أَن نَّذِلَّ وَنَخْزَىٰ (۱۳٤)
Had We destroyed them with a punishment before it, they would have surely said, ‘Our Lord! Why did You not send us an apostle so that we might have followed Your signs before we were abased and disgraced?’ (134)

Messages

1- The sending of the Prophets علیهم السلام is for the completion of the proof (Hujjah), so that people do not use their ignorance as an excuse. «لولا ارسلت...»

2- Until the law is announced, the penalty and punishment will not be just. «لولا ارسلت...»

3- Propagating the religion is obligatory, whether people pay attention or not. Because otherwise, they will have the right to object. «لولا ارسلت...»

4- Following the Prophets is the key to honor and dignity. «من قبل ان نذلّ و نخزى»

Ayah

قُلْ كُلٌّ مُّتَرَبِّصٌ فَتَرَبَّصُوا۟ ۖ فَسَتَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلصِّرَٰطِ ٱلسَّوِىِّ وَمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ (۱۳٥)
Say, ‘Everyone [of us] is waiting. So wait! Soon you will know who are the people of the right path, and who is [rightly] guided.’ (135)

Key Points

The meaning of "أصحاب الصراط السوّى" (Companions of the Straight Path) refers to the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام), and the meaning of "مَن اهتدى" (who is guided) refers to their helpers/supporters. (165) Imam Kadhim (علیه السلام) said regarding the Ayah, "مَن أصحاب الصراط السوىّ و مَن اهتدى": The straight path is the path of our Qā'im (علیه السلام), and the guided one is he who is guided to his obedience. (166)

165) Tafsir Furqan. 166) Ta'wil al-Ayat, p. 317.

Messages

1- The Prophet is commanded to warn and caution. "فتربّصوا"

2- The passage of time and history clarifies everything. "فستعلمون"

3- The disbelievers imagine themselves to be guided. "فستعلمون من اهتدى"

4- Islam is the school of moderation, and Muslims are the guided ones. "من أصحاب الصراط السوى و من اهتدى"
